# Outpost CMS — Developer Reference

> A developer-first headless CMS built on PHP + SQLite with a data-attribute template engine, a Svelte 5 admin SPA, and zero Composer dependencies. The admin panel, API, database, uploads, and cache all live inside a single `/outpost/` directory.

## Documentation

- [Full Developer Docs](/outpost/docs/): Complete reference for site structure, template syntax, collections, members, API, and deploy
- [Template Syntax Reference](/outpost/docs/template-syntax/): All template tags
- [Collection Loops](/outpost/docs/loops/collection-loops.html): Looping over collection items with options
- [Flexible Content Loops](/outpost/docs/loops/flexible-content-loops.html): Layout builder loops with named layouts
- [Relationship Loops](/outpost/docs/loops/relationship-loops.html): Linked content from other collections
- [Conditional Logic](/outpost/docs/features/conditional-logic.html): Show/hide fields based on other field values
- [Channels](/outpost/docs/features/channels.html): External data sources (REST API, RSS, CSV) with template loops
- [Channel Loops](/outpost/docs/loops/channel-loops.html): Template syntax for channel data loops (type-agnostic)
- [Members System](/outpost/docs/members/): Member registration, login, password reset
- [REST API Reference](/outpost/docs/api/): All admin and member API endpoints
- [GraphQL API](/outpost/docs/api/graphql.html): Full GraphQL API with queries, mutations, introspection, and interactive playground
- [Deploy Guide](/outpost/docs/deploy.html): Apache, Nginx, file permissions, cache
- [Brand Settings](/outpost/docs/features/brand.html): Site-wide colors, fonts, logo, and favicon — baseline visual identity
- [Auto-Updater](/outpost/docs/features/auto-updater.html): Built-in update system
- [Security](/outpost/docs/reference/security.html): Security hardening, rate limiting, SSRF prevention
- [Setup Wizard](/outpost/docs/features/setup-wizard.html): First-run setup wizard, content packs, getting started checklist, contextual tips
- [Forge](/outpost/docs/features/forge.html): Visual tag builder — select HTML, right-click or Cmd+E, wrap in template tags with smart detection
- [Content Packs](/outpost/docs/themes/content-packs.html): JSON content packs for seeding starter content
- [Ranger AI Assistant](/outpost/docs/features/ranger.html): AI-powered assistant with 35 tools, multi-provider support, and streaming chat
- [Releases](/outpost/docs/features/releases.html): Bundle multiple content changes and publish them all at once with one-click rollback
- [Collaboration & Comments](/outpost/docs/features/collaboration.html): Team comments, client review links, @mentions, and feedback overlay
- [Lodge](/outpost/docs/features/lodge.html): Member-owned content portals — let members create, edit, and manage their own collection items
- [Compass](/outpost/docs/features/compass.html): Smart filtering and search for any collection — 12 filter types, URL state sync, mobile flyout, indexed search
- [Schema Editor](/outpost/docs/features/schema-editor.html): Full-page collection schema editor with drag-sortable field builder, visual repeater sub-fields, and all 15 field types
- [Boost](/outpost/docs/features/boost.html): Performance optimization suite — page caching, browser cache headers, GZIP compression, HTML minification, lazy loading, database optimization, Developer Mode
- [Shield](/outpost/docs/features/shield.html): Security suite — login protection, IP blocklist, WAF-lite firewall, file integrity monitoring, security headers, traffic logging, email notifications
- [Redirects](/outpost/docs/features/redirects.html): URL redirect management — exact, wildcard, and regex matching, 301/302/307 types, CSV import, hit tracking, open-redirect prevention
- [Changelog](/outpost/docs/changelog.html): Every release and its changes
- [Roadmap](/outpost/docs/roadmap.html): What's shipped, what's next, and why

---

# Outpost CMS — Full Reference (llms-full)

## Stack

- PHP 8.x + SQLite (PDO) — no Composer, no frameworks
- Svelte 5 SPA (Vite 6) for the admin UI — built to `php/admin/`
- data-attribute template engine that compiles `.html` templates to PHP and caches them
- TipTap for rich text editing, SortableJS for drag-and-drop reorder

## Admin URL

The admin panel is at `/outpost/`. The `index.php` at the outpost root serves the Svelte SPA by loading `admin/index.html` and rewriting asset paths. There is no `/outpost/admin/` URL for end users — everything goes through `/outpost/`.

## File Structure

```
your-site/                       # OUTPOST_SITE_ROOT — the content source
  index.html                     # / route
  about.html                     # /about
  blog.html                      # /blog
  post.html                      # /post/{slug} single item
  partials/
    head.html                    # DOCTYPE through <body>
    nav.html                     # site navigation
    footer.html                  # </body></html>
  assets/
    style.css
  theme.json                     # Legacy — removed in v5.0
  .htaccess                      # Apache rewrite rules (auto-generated on install)
  outpost/                       # entire CMS engine
    admin/                       # built Svelte SPA (do not edit directly)
    content/
      data/
        cms.db                   # SQLite database
      uploads/                   # media files
      backups/                   # auto-created before each sync push
    cache/                       # compiled template cache + HTML page cache
    api.php                      # admin REST API
    member-api.php               # member REST API
    sync-api.php                 # builder sync API
    front-router.php             # PHP dev server router
    engine.php                   # core CMS functions (cms_text, cms_global, etc.)
    channels.php                 # channel engine: sync, fetch, cache, template functions
    template-engine-v2.php       # Template compiler (OutpostTemplate::render)
    mailer.php                   # SMTP / mail() email
    members.php                  # member session helpers (OutpostMember)
    ranger.php                   # Ranger AI assistant: tools, streaming, providers
    config.php                   # constants: DB path, OUTPOST_SITE_ROOT, etc.
    forge-analyze.php            # Forge site analysis engine
    docs/
      index.html                 # developer documentation
      llms.txt                   # this file
    member-pages/
      login.php
      register.php
      forgot-password.php
      reset-password.php
      verify-email.php
      logout.php
      profile.php
  index.php                      # Apache/Nginx front controller
```

**Key v5.0 change:** There is no theme layer. The site root (`OUTPOST_SITE_ROOT`) IS the content source. HTML templates, partials, and assets live directly at the site root, not inside `outpost/themes/`. The constant `OUTPOST_SITE_ROOT` is defined in `config.php` as `dirname(OUTPOST_DIR) . '/'`.

## Routing

Rules evaluated in order:
1. Static files (images, CSS, JS) — served directly, no PHP
2. `/outpost/` paths — served directly by outpost (admin, API, docs, etc.)
3. `/sitemap.xml` — auto-generated sitemap
4. Collection URL patterns (e.g. `/post/{slug}`) — router extracts slug, sets `$_GET['slug']`, renders the single template
5. Channel URL patterns (e.g. `/listing/{slug}`) — same as collections but for external data channels
6. Path maps to site root template file (e.g. `/about` → `about.html`)
7. No match → 404

URL pattern example: pattern `/post/{slug}` with URL `/post/my-article` → template `post.html`, `$_GET['slug'] = 'my-article'`

Draft blocking: items with `status='draft'` return 404. Preview: append `?preview=TOKEN` (from `items/preview-token` API) to bypass draft blocking with a secure per-item token. Visibility enforcement runs before rendering (see Protecting Pages).

### Writing links in templates (IMPORTANT)

Links in templates must use **route paths** (the URL visitors see), never template filenames:

| Correct | Wrong | Why |
|---|---|---|
| `<a href="/">` | `<a href="index.html">` | `index.html` is the template file, `/` is the URL |
| `<a href="/about">` | `<a href="about.html">` | `/about.html` is not a valid route — returns 404 |
| `<a href="/blog">` | `<a href="blog.html">` | The router maps `/blog` to `blog.html` internally |

In collection loops use `<span data-outpost="url"></span>` — Outpost generates correct URLs automatically. In menu loops use `<span data-outpost="url"></span>` — already contains the configured route path.

**When converting static HTML sites to Outpost sites, update every `<a href="page.html">` to `<a href="/page">`.**

### Defining custom routes

Outpost uses file-based routing — there is no route registration function, no config file, no middleware pipeline. Creating a template file IS defining a route:

| You create | The route becomes |
|---|---|
| `events.html` | `/events` |
| `pricing.html` | `/pricing` |
| `contact.html` | `/contact` |
| `docs.html` | `/docs` |

Dynamic routes use collections: create a collection with URL pattern `/event/{slug}` and a template `event.html`. The router matches `/event/my-event`, extracts the slug, and renders the template with `<outpost-single collection="event">`.

**What you cannot do:**
- Multi-segment static routes like `/docs/getting-started` — template files map 1:1 to single-segment paths. Use query params (`/docs?page=getting-started`) or collection URL patterns instead.
- Custom PHP route handlers — the router is not extensible. For custom server-side logic, create a standalone PHP file (e.g. `/custom-api.php`) outside the CMS.
- Route parameters beyond `{slug}` — collections and channels support only `{slug}` in URL patterns.

### Adding a new route

To add a route for `/services`, create `services.html` at the site root. No config or PHP needed — the router checks the filesystem on every request.

### Custom PHP routes (standalone scripts)

For pages needing server-side logic, create a standalone PHP file that bootstraps Outpost:

```php
<?php
// Minimum bootstrap for a standalone PHP file
$outpostDir = __DIR__ . '/outpost';
require_once $outpostDir . '/config.php';     // Constants: OUTPOST_DIR, OUTPOST_SITE_ROOT, etc.
require_once $outpostDir . '/db.php';          // OutpostDB (static PDO wrapper)
require_once $outpostDir . '/engine.php';      // Template helpers + outpost_init()

// Site root is the content source (v5: no theme layer)
$siteRoot = OUTPOST_SITE_ROOT;

// Load template engine (auto-detect v1 or v2)
$useV2 = outpost_detect_engine_version($siteRoot);
require_once $outpostDir . ($useV2 ? '/template-engine-v2.php' : '/template-engine.php');

// Your custom logic here
$query = trim($_GET['q'] ?? '');
$results = OutpostDB::fetchAll(
    "SELECT * FROM collection_items WHERE status = 'published' AND json_extract(data, '$.title') LIKE ? LIMIT 20",
    ['%' . $query . '%']
);

// Initialize and render a template from the site root
outpost_init();
outpost_render_template($siteRoot . 'search.html', $siteRoot);
```

Bootstrap files: `config.php` (constants), `db.php` (OutpostDB), `engine.php` (template helpers + outpost_init()), `template-engine.php` or `template-engine-v2.php` (compiler), `members.php` (optional, for gated pages).

Route the custom PHP file via Apache rewrite (`RewriteRule ^search$ search.php [L]`), Nginx location block, or by adding a check in `front-router.php`.

### Asset paths must be absolute

Always use absolute paths starting with `/` for CSS, JS, images, and fonts:
- Correct: `/assets/style.css`
- Wrong: `assets/style.css` (breaks on `/about`, `/post/hello-world`, etc.)

Relative paths resolve differently per page URL. On `/about`, `assets/style.css` becomes `/about/assets/style.css` — a 404.

## theme.json (Removed in v5.0)

> **Removed in v5.0.** The theme layer no longer exists. The following section is retained for reference only if maintaining pre-v5 sites.

An optional `theme.json` at the site root previously provided metadata and enabled the CSS framework.

```json
{
  "name": "My Site",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "author": "Your Name",
  "description": "A clean personal blog.",
  "framework": true
}
```

| Field | Required | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `name` | No | Display name in admin |
| `version` | No | Semantic version |
| `author` | No | Creator name |
| `description` | No | Short summary |
| `framework` | No | When `true`, injects `--brand-*` CSS tokens and framework CSS before `</head>` |

### Brand Settings

Brand (Admin → Brand) sets site-wide visual identity: palette (primary, secondary, accent, neutral, background, surface), typography (heading font, body font, type scale ratio), and identity (logo, favicon). Stored in `content/data/brand.json`.

When `"framework": true` is set in `theme.json`, brand values are injected as `--brand-*` CSS custom properties before `</head>`. Site CSS uses `var(--brand-primary)`, `var(--brand-font-heading)`, etc.

Available brand keys: `colors.primary`, `colors.secondary`, `colors.accent`, `colors.neutral`, `colors.background`, `colors.surface`, `typography.heading_font`, `typography.body_font`, `identity.logo`, `identity.favicon`.

#### Custom Google Fonts

The font picker in Brand ships with ~30 curated Google Fonts. Admins can add any Google Font via Brand → Typography → "Manage fonts". Enter the exact font name from fonts.google.com and choose a category (Sans Serif, Serif, Display, Monospace). Custom fonts are stored in the `settings` table as the `custom_fonts` key (JSON array of `{ name, category }` objects) and automatically appear in all font picker dropdowns.

API: `GET api.php?action=fonts` returns `{ fonts: [...] }` (custom fonts only). `PUT api.php?action=fonts` with body `{ fonts: [{ name, category }, ...] }` saves them. Requires `settings.*` capability.

## Template Scanning & Auto-Rescan

`outpost_scan_theme_templates()` parses all `.html` template files at `OUTPOST_SITE_ROOT` and:
1. Creates page stubs in the `pages` table for each template
2. Creates field stubs in the `fields` table (never overwrites saved content)
3. Upserts into `page_field_registry` with field type, default value, and sort order

The scanner runs:
- On first request when no fields exist
- **Automatically on each frontend request** when any template file (top-level `.html` or `partials/*.html`) has been modified since the last scan

Auto-rescan uses `filemtime()` comparison against a `last_template_scan` setting stored in the `settings` table. Editing templates via the code editor or direct file access triggers an automatic re-scan on the next frontend request.

The scanner extracts defaults from all syntax forms:
- Inline: `<span data-outpost="field">Default</span>`, `<span data-outpost="field" data-type="type">Default</span>`
- Closing-tag: `<span data-outpost="field">Default</span>`, `data-outpost="field" data-type="raw"Default HTML</div>`
- Global closing-tag: `<span data-outpost="field" data-scope="global"></span>Default</span>`, `<span data-outpost="field" data-scope="global" data-type="type"></span>Default</span>`

## Template Syntax — Complete Reference

### Output Tags

All output tags register the field in the admin page editor automatically.

```html
<span data-outpost="field_name"></span>              <!-- escaped text (default) -->
<span data-outpost="field_name"></span>Default    <!-- with wrapping default value -->
<div data-outpost="field_name" data-type="richtext"></div>        <!-- HTML as-is (rich text, TipTap output) -->
<div data-outpost="field_name" data-type="richtext"></div><p>Default HTML</p>  <!-- richtext with wrapping default -->
<img data-outpost="field_name" data-type="image">      <!-- image URL, opens media picker in admin -->
<a data-outpost="field_name" data-type="link" href="#">Link</a>       <!-- URL value, opens link input -->
<p data-outpost="field_name" data-type="textarea"></p>   <!-- multi-line text -->
<span data-outpost="field_name" data-type="select"></span>     <!-- dropdown selector -->
data-outpost="field_name" data-type="color"      <!-- color picker -->
data-outpost="field_name" data-type="number"     <!-- numeric input -->
data-outpost="field_name" data-type="date"       <!-- date input -->
data-outpost="field_name" data-type="toggle"     <!-- boolean toggle -->
<p data-outpost="field_name" data-type="or_body"></p>    <!-- text/excerpt fallback: uses auto-excerpt from body field if field is empty -->
data-outpost="field_name" data-type="focal"      <!-- focal point: outputs "X% Y%" for object-position/background-position -->
data-outpost="field_name" data-edit       <!-- opt-in on-page editing (text field, editable on frontend) -->
data-outpost="field_name" data-type="richtext" data-edit <!-- richtext with on-page editing -->
data-outpost="field_name" data-type="image" data-edit  <!-- image with on-page editing -->
data-outpost="global_name" data-scope="global" data-edit     <!-- global field with on-page editing -->
```

**Filter purpose:** Filters like `| image`, `| link`, `| textarea` do NOT transform the output. They tell the admin page editor which input type to render for that field (media picker, URL input, multiline textarea, etc.). The template output is always the stored value. `data-outpost="hero" data-type="image"` outputs the image URL string — wrap it in `<img src="...">` yourself.

**On-page editing (`data-edit`):** Append `data-edit` to any field tag to enable on-page editing for that field. When a logged-in admin views the site, fields with `data-edit` become click-to-edit. Fields without `data-edit` are only editable in the admin panel. Works with text, textarea, richtext, image, and globals. The field scanner strips `data-edit` before type detection, so `data-outpost="hero" data-type="image" data-edit` registers as an image field.

Both inline (`<span data-outpost="field">Default</span>`) and closing-tag (`<span data-outpost="field">Default</span>`) default syntax is fully supported. Closing-tag defaults also work with filters: `data-outpost="field" data-type="raw"<p>HTML</p>`. Defaults are extracted by the template scanner and stored in `page_field_registry.default_value`, so the admin page editor pre-fills fields (including WYSIWYG richtext) with the template default when content is empty.

### Global Fields `data-outpost="@field"`

Site-wide settings shared across all pages. Appear in Admin → Globals.

```html
<span data-outpost="site_name" data-scope="global"></span>
<span data-outpost="site_name" data-scope="global" data-type="raw"></span>
<img data-outpost="logo" data-scope="global" data-type="image">
<span data-outpost="footer_text" data-scope="global" data-type="textarea"></span>
```

### Meta Tags

```html
<span data-outpost="title"></span>Default Site Title
<span data-outpost="description"></span>Default description.
```

Outputs `<title>` and `<meta name="description">` tags.

On single item pages (`<outpost-single>`): `meta.title` automatically falls back to the item's `title` field if no page-level meta title is set. `meta.description` auto-generates a 30-word excerpt from the item's `body` field if no page-level meta description is set.

### Collection Loops

```html
<outpost-each collection="post">
  <span data-outpost="title"></span>
  <span data-outpost="slug"></span>
  <span data-outpost="url"></span>             <!-- built from collection url_pattern -->
  <span data-outpost="id"></span>              <!-- integer database ID -->
  <span data-outpost="published_at"></span>
  <span data-outpost="created_at"></span>
  <span data-outpost="updated_at"></span>
  <div data-outpost="body" data-type="richtext"></div>      <!-- rich text -->
  <span data-outpost="any_field"></span>       <!-- any custom field -->
<outpost-else>
  No posts found.
</outpost-each>
```

### Loop Options (space-separated after collection name)

| Option | Example | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `limit:N` | `limit:5` | Max N items |
| `orderby:field` | `orderby:published_at` | Order by field — always descending (DESC) |
| `paginate:N` | `paginate:10` | N items per page, enables `<outpost-pagination />` |
| `filteredby:param` | `filteredby:category` | Filter by `?param=slug` query string |
| `related:var` | `related:post` | Items from same collection excluding current single item |

Examples:
```html
<outpost-each collection="post" limit:3 orderby:published_at>
<outpost-each collection="post" paginate:10 orderby:published_at filteredby:category>
<outpost-each collection="post" limit:3 related:post>
```

`paginate:N` and `limit:N` are mutually exclusive.

### Pagination

Place `<outpost-pagination />` after a loop that uses `paginate:N`. Renders `<nav>` with numbered links, Prev/Next, ellipsis. Preserves all query params (e.g. `?category=coding&page=2`).

```html
<outpost-each collection="post" paginate:10>
  ...
</outpost-each>

<outpost-pagination />
```

CSS classes: `.pagination`, `.page-link`, `.page-link.page-current`, `.page-ellipsis`

### Single Items

```html
<outpost-single collection="post">
  <span data-outpost="title"></span>
  <div data-outpost="body" data-type="richtext"></div>
<outpost-else>
  Not found.
</outpost-single>
```

Fetches by `?slug=` (set automatically by router). Related items inside a single:

```html
<outpost-single collection="post">
  <outpost-each collection="post" limit:3 related:post>
    <span data-outpost="title"></span>
  </outpost-each>
</outpost-single>
```

### Channel Loops

```html
<outpost-each channel="listings">
  <span data-outpost="title"></span>
  <span data-outpost="description"></span>
  <span data-outpost="price"></span>
<outpost-else>
  No listings available.
</outpost-each>
```

`listings` is the channel slug. Loop options: `limit:N` (max items), `orderby:field` (sort descending). Items come from cached external data. Syntax is type-agnostic — works the same for API, RSS, and CSV channels.

RSS feed example:
```html
<outpost-each channel="blog_feed" limit:10>
  <h2><a href="<span data-outpost="link"></span>"><span data-outpost="title"></span></a></h2>
  <time><span data-outpost="pubDate"></span></time>
  <p><span data-outpost="description"></span></p>
</outpost-each>
```

CSV example:
```html
<outpost-each channel="team">
  <h3><span data-outpost="first_name"></span></h3>
  <p><span data-outpost="role"></span></p>
</outpost-each>
```

### Channel Single Items

```html
<outpost-single channel="listings">
  <span data-outpost="title"></span>
  <span data-outpost="description"></span>
<outpost-else>
  Listing not found.
</outpost-single>
```

Fetches by `?slug=` (set by channel URL pattern routing). Works identically to collection `<outpost-single>`.

### Navigation Loops

```html
<outpost-menu name="main">
  <span data-outpost="label"></span>
  <span data-outpost="url"></span>
  <span data-outpost="target"></span>          <!-- _self or _blank -->
  <outpost-each item="children">
    <span data-outpost="label"></span>
    <span data-outpost="url"></span>
  </outpost-each>
</outpost-each>
```

`main` is the menu slug. Menu items support one level of nesting via `item.children`.

### Gallery Loops

```html
<outpost-each gallery="photos">
  <span data-outpost="url"></span>
  <span data-outpost="alt"></span>
  <span data-outpost="caption"></span>
</outpost-each>
```

`photos` is the gallery field name on the current page or item.

### Repeater Loops

```html
<outpost-each repeater="team_members">
  <span data-outpost="name"></span>
  <span data-outpost="role"></span>
  <span data-outpost="photo" data-type="image"></span>
</outpost-each>
```

`team_members` is the repeater field name. Each row has the sub-fields defined in the repeater schema.

### Flexible Content Loops

Flexible Content fields are ACF Pro-style layout builders. Define named layouts (e.g. Hero, CTA, Testimonial) each with their own sub-fields. Editors add, remove, and reorder layout blocks.

```html
<outpost-each flexible="page_sections">
  <outpost-if field="layout" equals="hero">
    <section class="hero">
      <h1><span data-outpost="heading"></span></h1>
      <p><span data-outpost="subheading"></span></p>
    </section>
  </outpost-if>
  <outpost-if field="layout" equals="cta">
    <section class="cta">
      <h2><span data-outpost="title"></span></h2>
      <a href="<span data-outpost="button_url"></span>"><span data-outpost="button_text"></span></a>
    </section>
  </outpost-if>
</outpost-each>
```

`page_sections` is the flexible content field name. Each block has a `block.layout` property identifying the layout type, plus all sub-fields defined for that layout. Sub-field values are auto-escaped (except richtext).

**Layout definition:** Layouts are defined in the collection schema builder as a JSON object. Each key is a layout name, each value is an object of sub-field definitions:

```json
{
  "hero": {
    "heading": { "type": "text", "label": "Heading" },
    "subheading": { "type": "textarea", "label": "Subheading" },
    "bg_image": { "type": "image", "label": "Background Image" }
  },
  "cta": {
    "title": { "type": "text", "label": "Title" },
    "button_text": { "type": "text", "label": "Button Text" },
    "button_url": { "type": "link", "label": "Button URL" }
  }
}
```

Supported sub-field types: text, textarea, richtext, image, link, select, color, number, date, toggle.

**Storage:** Flexible content is stored as a JSON array in the item's `data` column. Each entry has a `_layout` key (mapped to `block.layout` in templates) plus sub-field values.

**Engine function:** `cms_flexible_items(string $name): array` — decodes the JSON, escapes sub-field values (except `_layout` which becomes `layout`), returns an array of associative arrays.

### Relationship Loops

Relationship fields link content to items from another collection. Search and select items with reorder support. Stores item IDs, resolves full item data at render time.

```html
<outpost-each relationship="related_posts">
  <a href="<span data-outpost="url"></span>">
    <h3><span data-outpost="title"></span></h3>
    <p><span data-outpost="excerpt"></span></p>
    <time><span data-outpost="published_at"></span></time>
  </a>
</outpost-each>
```

`related_posts` is the relationship field name. Each item has all standard collection item fields: `id`, `slug`, `url`, `published_at`, `created_at`, `updated_at`, plus all custom fields defined in the related collection's schema.

**Schema options:**
- `collection` — slug of the collection to pull items from
- `multiple` — `true` (default) for multi-select, `false` for single item
- `max` — maximum number of items (0 = unlimited)

**Storage:** Stored as a JSON array of item IDs (integers) in the item's `data` column. Items are resolved at render time via a database JOIN — only published items are returned, in the order they were selected.

**Engine function:** `cms_relationship_items(string $name): array` — decodes the ID array, fetches full item records with collection URL patterns, escapes field values, returns ordered array of associative arrays.

### Folder Loops

```html
<outpost-each folder="categories">
  <span data-outpost="name"></span>
  <span data-outpost="slug"></span>
  <span data-outpost="description"></span>
  <span data-outpost="count"></span>           <!-- number of published items with this label -->
<outpost-else>
  <p>No categories yet.</p>
</outpost-each>
```

### `<outpost-else>` on All Loops

All loop types support `<outpost-else>` for empty-state fallbacks (v1.9.3). This includes: collection, channel, folder, menu, gallery, media folder, repeater, flexible content, and relationship loops. The `<outpost-else>` block renders when the loop has zero items.

### Conditionals

```html
<outpost-if field="field_name" exists>...</outpost-if>
<outpost-if field="@global_name" exists>...</outpost-if>
<outpost-if field="field" exists>...</outpost-if>
<outpost-if field="field" equals="value">...</outpost-if>
<outpost-if field="field" not-equals="value">...</outpost-if>
<outpost-if field="field" exists>...<outpost-else>...</outpost-if>
<outpost-if field="admin" exists>...</outpost-if>   <!-- true only when previewing in the admin editor -->
```

**Conditional limitations:** Outpost conditionals do not support `&&` (AND), `||` (OR), or `!` (NOT) operators. Each `<outpost-if>` tests a single expression. For compound logic, nest `<outpost-if>` blocks. There is no `elseif` — use nested `<outpost-if>` inside `<outpost-else>`.

### Includes

```html
<outpost-include partial="head" />     <!-- loads partials/head.html -->
<outpost-include partial="nav" />
<outpost-include partial="footer" />
<outpost-include partial="sidebar" />
<outpost-include partial="components/card" />  <!-- loads partials/components/card.html -->
```

### Comments

```html
<!-- This is stripped at compile time — never appears in HTML -->
```

### Full Tag Reference

| Tag | Description |
|---|---|
| `data-outpost="field"` | Output field value (escaped) |
| `data-outpost="field" data-type="raw"` | Rich text / HTML unescaped |
| `data-outpost="field" data-type="image"` | Image picker |
| `data-outpost="field" data-type="link"` | Link input |
| `data-outpost="field" data-type="textarea"` | Multiline text |
| `data-outpost="field" data-type="select"` | Dropdown |
| `data-outpost="field" data-type="color"` | Color picker |
| `data-outpost="field" data-type="number"` | Number input |
| `data-outpost="field" data-type="date"` | Date input |
| `data-outpost="field" data-type="toggle"` | Boolean toggle |
| `data-outpost="field" data-type="or_body"` | Text with auto-excerpt fallback from body |
| `data-outpost="field" data-type="focal"` | Focal point as CSS `X% Y%` for `object-position` |
| `data-outpost="field" data-type="edit"` | Opt-in on-page editing (append to any type: `data-outpost="field" data-type="image" data-edit`) |
| `data-outpost="@global"` | Output global field |
| `<span data-outpost="title"></span>Default` | `<title>` tag (legacy — use `<outpost-seo />` in new sites) |
| `<span data-outpost="description"></span>Default` | `<meta name="description">` tag (legacy — use `<outpost-seo />` in new sites) |
| `<outpost-seo />` | Full SEO block: title, description, OG, Twitter Card, canonical, JSON-LD, robots |
| `<outpost-each collection="slug">` | Collection loop |
| `<outpost-single collection="slug">` | Single item by slug |
| `<outpost-each folder="slug">` | Folder label loop |
| `<outpost-menu name="slug">` | Navigation menu loop |
| `<outpost-each gallery="field">` | Gallery images loop |
| `<outpost-each repeat="field">` | Repeater rows loop |
| `<outpost-each flexible="field">` | Flexible content layout blocks loop |
| `<outpost-each relationship="field">` | Relationship items loop |
| `<outpost-pagination />` | Pagination nav |
| `<outpost-if field="expr" exists>` | Conditional |
| `<outpost-if field="admin" exists>` | True in admin editor preview |
| `<outpost-else>` | Else branch |
| `</outpost-if>` / `</outpost-each>` / `</outpost-single>` | Close blocks |
| `<outpost-include partial="name" />` | Insert partial |
| `<!-- comment -->` | Template comment (stripped) |

## Template Engine v2 — Data Attribute Syntax

### Engine Detection

Themes are automatically detected as v2 if `index.html` contains `data-outpost=` or `<outpost-` patterns. No configuration needed — the engine is selected per-site at render time.

### Fields

Define editable fields on any HTML element using `data-outpost`:

```html
<!-- Plain text field -->
<h1 data-outpost="headline">Default Headline</h1>

<!-- Typed fields -->
<img data-outpost="hero_image" data-type="image" src="placeholder.jpg" />
<div data-outpost="body" data-type="richtext">Default body content</div>
<a data-outpost="cta_link" data-type="link" href="#">Learn More</a>
<textarea data-outpost="bio" data-type="textarea">About me</textarea>
<input data-outpost="subscribe" data-type="toggle" />
<select data-outpost="layout" data-type="select"></select>
<span data-outpost="price" data-type="number">0</span>
<time data-outpost="published" data-type="date">2025-01-01</time>
<div data-outpost="accent" data-type="color">#ff0000</div>

<!-- Global field (shared across all pages) -->
<span data-outpost="site_name" data-scope="global">My Site</span>
```

Supported `data-type` values: `image`, `richtext`, `link`, `textarea`, `toggle`, `select`, `number`, `date`, `color`. Omit `data-type` for plain text.

Use `data-scope="global"` to read from global field storage instead of page-scoped fields.

### Block Grouping

Group fields into named sections using HTML comments:

```html
<!-- outpost:hero -->
<section class="hero">
  <h1 data-outpost="title">Welcome</h1>
  <p data-outpost="subtitle">A brief intro</p>
</section>
<!-- /outpost:hero -->

<!-- outpost:about global -->
<section class="about">
  <p data-outpost="company_bio" data-type="richtext">About us</p>
</section>
<!-- /outpost:about -->
```

- `<!-- outpost:blockname -->...<!-- /outpost:blockname -->` groups fields into editable sections.
- `<!-- outpost:blockname global -->` marks the block as global (fields shared across all pages).

### Block Settings

Attach per-block settings using a special comment before the block element:

```html
<!-- outpost-settings:
    bg_color: color(#ffffff)
    layout: select(centered, left-aligned)
    show_cta: toggle(true)
-->
<section class="hero">
  ...
</section>
```

Settings output as CSS custom properties and data attributes on the next sibling element. For example, `bg_color: color(#ffffff)` becomes `style="--bg_color: #ffffff"` and `data-bg_color="#ffffff"` on the `<section>`.

Each settings line must follow the format `field_name: type(args)`. Field names may contain letters, numbers, underscores, and hyphens (e.g. `bg-color: color(#fff)`). Malformed lines that don't match this format are silently ignored by the parser.

### Collection Loops

```html
<!-- List items from a collection -->
<outpost-each collection="posts" limit="6" sort="published_at" order="desc">
  <article>
    <h2 data-outpost="title">Post Title</h2>
    <p data-outpost="excerpt">Summary text</p>
    <img data-outpost="featured_image" data-type="image" />
  </article>
</outpost-each>

<!-- Empty state fallback -->
<outpost-each collection="posts" empty>
  <p>No posts yet.</p>
</outpost-each>
```

### Repeater, Gallery, and Folder Loops

```html
<!-- Repeater -->
<outpost-each repeat="skills">
  <li data-outpost="skill">Skill name</li>
</outpost-each>

<!-- Gallery -->
<outpost-each gallery="photos">
  <img data-outpost="image" data-type="image" />
</outpost-each>

<!-- Folder / taxonomy -->
<outpost-each folder="categories">
  <a data-outpost="label" data-bind="href:url">Category</a>
</outpost-each>
```

### Single Item

```html
<!-- Render a single collection item by URL slug -->
<outpost-single collection="posts">
  <article>
    <h1 data-outpost="title">Post Title</h1>
    <div data-outpost="body" data-type="richtext">Content here</div>
  </article>
</outpost-single>

<!-- Not-found fallback -->
<outpost-single collection="posts" else>
  <p>Post not found.</p>
</outpost-single>
```

### Conditionals

```html
<outpost-if field="featured_image" exists>
  <img data-outpost="featured_image" data-type="image" />
</outpost-if>

<outpost-if field="status" is="published">
  <span class="badge">Live</span>
</outpost-if>

<outpost-if field="site_name" scope="global" exists>
  <span data-outpost="site_name" data-scope="global">Site</span>
</outpost-if>
```

Supported operators: `is`, `equals`, `not`, `exists`, `empty`.

### Includes, Menus, and SEO

```html
<!-- Include a partial from partials/ directory -->
<outpost-include partial="nav" />

<!-- Navigation menu -->
<outpost-menu name="main">
  <a data-bind="href:url" data-outpost="label">Link</a>
</outpost-menu>
```

Menu items expose `label`, `url`, and `target` fields.

```html
<!-- Full SEO block (title, meta, OG, JSON-LD, etc.) -->
<outpost-seo />

<!-- Or manual SEO meta -->
<outpost-meta title="My Page" description="Page description here" />
```

### Pagination

```html
<outpost-pagination />
```

Outputs pagination controls for collection loops.

### Attribute Binding

Inside loops, bind item data to HTML attributes:

```html
<outpost-each collection="posts">
  <time data-bind="datetime:published_at" data-outpost="published_at">Jan 1</time>
  <a data-bind="href:url" data-outpost="title">Post Title</a>
  <img data-bind="src:featured_image" data-bind="alt:title" />
</outpost-each>
```

`data-bind="attr:field"` sets the specified HTML attribute from the item's field value.

### Auto-Hide

Elements with `data-outpost` that resolve to an empty value are automatically removed from output. No explicit conditionals needed for optional fields — if the field has no content, the element is stripped.

### Output Modes

- **Public rendering** — all Outpost syntax (`data-outpost`, `data-type`, `data-scope`, `data-bind`, `<outpost-*>` elements, `<!-- outpost: -->` comments) is stripped from output. The result is clean, semantic HTML.
- **Editor rendering** — when the page is loaded in the editor iframe with `?_outpost_editor=1`, all `data-outpost` attributes and `data-outpost-block` attributes are preserved in output so the click-to-edit bridge can map elements back to their fields.

### Browse / Edit Mode

The editor has two distinct modes:

- **Browse mode (default)** — the site works like a normal website. Links navigate normally, no editing UI is active. This is the mode when the editor first loads or when the edit drawer is closed.
- **Edit mode** — activated by clicking the Edit (pencil) icon in the sidebar rail, which opens the Edit drawer. In edit mode, clicking any `data-outpost` element in the preview selects the corresponding field in the drawer instead of navigating. Closing the drawer returns to browse mode.

### Click-to-Edit Bridge (Editor Mode)

The click-to-edit bridge connects the editor preview iframe to the sidebar field panels. It is automatic and requires no developer action.

- **Activation**: Bridge JS is injected before `</body>` when the page is loaded with `?_outpost_editor=1` query param (editor iframe only; never on the live site).
- **Hover outlines**: Moving the mouse over any `[data-outpost]` element shows a dotted outline and a small label with the field name.
- **Click field**: Clicking a `[data-outpost]` element sends a `postMessage` to the parent admin frame, which scrolls the sidebar to that field and highlights it.
- **Click block background**: Clicking an element with `[data-outpost-block]` (or the background area of a block) opens the block panel in the sidebar showing all fields in that block.
- **Reverse highlighting**: When hovering over a field in the sidebar, the corresponding element in the preview iframe is highlighted with an outline. The sidebar sends a message to the iframe, and the bridge JS adds a highlight class to the matching element.
- **Block attribute injection**: In editor mode, the v2 engine adds `data-outpost-block="name"` to the first element after each `<!-- outpost:name -->` block comment, enabling block-level click targeting.

### Storyblok-style EditDrawer

The EditDrawer is the sidebar editing panel in the frontend visual editor. It provides a three-level drill-down UI for editing page content:

1. **Section list** — shows all blocks on the page (hero, features, testimonials, etc.) as clickable rows. Each row shows the block name and a count of editable fields.
2. **Section detail** — clicking a section opens its detail view with two tabs: **General** (all editable content fields in the block) and **Style** (outpost-settings fields like colors, layout variants, toggles, ranges). Fields are rendered inline with live preview updates.
3. **Field detail** — clicking a field opens a focused editing view for that specific field (richtext editor, image picker, link editor, etc.).

**Breadcrumb navigation** at the top of the drawer shows the current drill-down path (e.g., "Sections > Hero > Title") and allows clicking any level to navigate back.

**Bridge integration**: When a user clicks an element in the preview iframe, the bridge JS sends a postMessage identifying the block and field. The EditDrawer automatically navigates to the matching section and scrolls to/highlights the corresponding field.

### Section Comment Contract

The `<!-- outpost:section -->` comment syntax groups fields into named editable sections in the admin editor:

```html
<!-- outpost:hero -->
<section class="hero">
  <h1 data-outpost="hero_title">Welcome</h1>
  <p data-outpost="hero_subtitle">Subtitle</p>
</section>
<!-- /outpost:hero -->
```

- `<!-- outpost:name -->...<!-- /outpost:name -->` groups fields into editable sections
- `<!-- outpost:name global -->` marks the section as global (fields shared across all pages)
- The admin editor shows sections as collapsible groups with all their fields

### Navigation Element Contract

Outpost recognizes `<nav>` elements for menu integration. When Forge analyzes a site, `<nav>` elements with links are automatically wrapped with `<outpost-menu>` loops.

### v2 Quick Reference

| Syntax | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `data-outpost="name"` | Editable field |
| `data-type="image\|richtext\|..."` | Field type hint |
| `data-scope="global"` | Global field |
| `<!-- outpost:block -->` | Block group open |
| `<!-- /outpost:block -->` | Block group close |
| `<!-- outpost:block global -->` | Global block |
| `<!-- outpost-settings: ... -->` | Block settings |
| `<outpost-each collection="slug">` | Collection loop |
| `<outpost-each collection="slug" empty>` | Empty fallback |
| `<outpost-each repeat="name">` | Repeater loop |
| `<outpost-each gallery="name">` | Gallery loop |
| `<outpost-each folder="slug">` | Folder/taxonomy loop |
| `<outpost-single collection="slug">` | Single item |
| `<outpost-single collection="slug" else>` | Not-found fallback |
| `<outpost-if field="x" exists>` | Conditional |
| `<outpost-if field="x" scope="global">` | Global conditional |
| `<outpost-include partial="name" />` | Include partial |
| `<outpost-menu name="slug">` | Menu loop |
| `<outpost-seo />` | Full SEO output |
| `<outpost-meta title="..." />` | SEO meta |
| `<outpost-pagination />` | Pagination controls |
| `data-bind="attr:field"` | Attribute binding |

## Partials

Stored in `partials/` at the site root. Included via `<outpost-include partial="name" />`. Subdirectories supported: `<outpost-include partial="components/card" />` maps to `partials/components/card.html`. Each partial inherits the full template context from its parent.

Standard partial names: `head` (DOCTYPE through `<body>`), `nav` (navigation), `footer` (closing tags).

**Recommended `head.html`:** Use `<outpost-seo />` instead of manual `<span data-outpost="title"></span>` / `<span data-outpost="description"></span>` tags. The `<outpost-seo />` tag auto-generates `<title>`, meta description, Open Graph, Twitter Card, canonical URL, JSON-LD Article schema, and pagination links with a fallback chain:

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8">
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
  <outpost-seo />
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="/assets/style.css">
</head>
<body>
```

## Site Assets

Static files in `assets/` at the site root are served directly by the web server. Use absolute paths in templates:

```html
<link rel="stylesheet" href="/assets/style.css">
```

No asset pipeline or build step. Cache busting: add `?v=2` to URLs.

## Collections

A collection = a type of repeating content (posts, team members, projects, etc.).

Each collection has:
- `name` — display name (e.g. "Blog Posts")
- `slug` — used in templates (e.g. `post`) — immutable after creation
- `singular_name` — used in admin UI
- `url_pattern` — e.g. `/post/{slug}` — builds item URLs and routes single pages
- `schema` — field definitions

**Field types:** text, textarea, richtext, image, link, select, color, number, date, toggle, repeater, gallery, flexible, relationship.

**Setup workflow:** Unlike pages (where creating `about.html` instantly makes `/about` work), collections require two steps:
1. Create the collection in Admin → Collections (set name, slug, URL pattern, add schema fields)
2. Create the template files — a list page (e.g. `blog.html`) and a single page (e.g. `post.html`). The single template filename **must match the collection slug**: slug `post` → `post.html`, slug `portfolio` → `portfolio.html`.

If the template exists but the collection hasn't been created in admin, `<outpost-each collection="post">` renders nothing (no error). If the collection exists but the template doesn't, visiting an item URL returns 404.

Items have statuses: `published`, `draft`, `scheduled`, `pending_review`. Only `published` items with `published_at` in the past appear in loops and single templates. Items with `pending_review` status are treated as drafts (not visible to visitors).

Single template naming: URL pattern `/post/{slug}` → template file `post.html` (first path segment).

### Conditional Logic

Collection schema fields support conditional logic — show/hide fields based on other field values. Conditions are evaluated in real-time in the editor; hidden fields are not rendered.

**Operators:**
| Operator | Description |
|---|---|
| `==` | Equals (string compare; also matches `true`/`1` and `false`/`0`/empty) |
| `!=` | Not equal |
| `not_empty` | Has any value (not empty, not `0`, not `false`) |
| `empty` | Is empty, `0`, or `false` |

**Schema definition:** Conditions are stored per-field in the collection's `schema` JSON:

```json
{
  "category": { "type": "select", "label": "Category", "options": "news,event,product" },
  "event_date": {
    "type": "date",
    "label": "Event Date",
    "conditions": [
      { "field": "category", "operator": "==", "value": "event" }
    ]
  }
}
```

When `category` is not "event", the `event_date` field is hidden from the editor. Multiple conditions use AND logic (all must be true). Conditional logic is editor-side only — it does not affect template rendering or API output.

## Collection Schema Editor (v4.6.10+)

A full-page editor for collection schemas, accessed via Admin → Collections → Edit on any collection. Route: `collection-schema`.

### What it replaces

Previously, collection schema editing was done in a small inline modal. The new schema editor is a full-page view with room for all settings and fields.

### Collection settings

The top of the page contains collection-level settings:

| Setting | Description |
|---|---|
| Name | Display name (e.g. "Blog Posts") |
| Slug | URL-safe identifier, used in templates and API (immutable after creation) |
| URL pattern | Route pattern for single items, e.g. `/post/{slug}` |
| Sort field | Default sort column for items (created_at, updated_at, published_at, sort_order) |
| Items per page | Pagination limit for collection loops |

### Schema field types (15 total)

| Type | Description |
|---|---|
| text | Single-line text input |
| textarea | Multi-line plain text |
| richtext | TipTap rich text editor with formatting toolbar |
| image | Image picker with media library integration |
| link | URL field with optional label |
| select | Dropdown with configurable choices |
| color | Native color picker |
| number | Numeric input |
| date | Date/datetime picker |
| toggle | Boolean on/off switch |
| repeater | Nested repeating rows with sub-fields |
| gallery | Sortable image grid with add/remove/reorder |
| flexible | Flexible content layouts (ACF Pro-style) |
| relationship | Linked items from another collection |
| conditional | Any field can have show/hide conditions |

### Drag-sortable field builder

Fields are displayed as rows that can be reordered via drag-and-drop (SortableJS). Each row shows the field slug, type badge, and expand/collapse controls. Expanded view shows type-specific options.

### Visual repeater sub-field builder

Repeater fields get a nested builder where each sub-field has:
- **Name** — the sub-field slug (e.g. `skill`, `level`)
- **Type** — any of the 15 field types
- **Label** — display label in the editor

A "Switch to JSON" toggle reveals the raw JSON definition for advanced editing. The visual builder and JSON editor stay in sync.

### Type-specific options

- **Select** — choices editor (comma-separated or one per line)
- **Relationship** — collection picker dropdown to select which collection to link
- **Flexible** — layouts JSON editor for defining named layouts with fields
- **Repeater** — visual sub-field builder (see above)

### Conditional logic

Any field can have conditions that control visibility in the editor. Conditions reference other fields in the same schema:

- **Operators:** `==` (equals), `!=` (not equals), `not_empty`, `empty`
- **Negation:** "NOT" modifier inverts any condition
- **Multiple conditions** use AND logic (all must be true to show the field)

### Lodge settings panel

For Lodge-enabled collections, the schema editor includes a Lodge configuration section with:
- Enable/disable Lodge
- Allow create/edit/delete toggles
- Require approval toggle
- Max items per member
- Editable and read-only field whitelists
- Required tiers

### Additional features

- **Require review toggle** — enable editorial review for the collection directly from the schema editor
- **Keyboard shortcut** — Cmd+S (Mac) or Ctrl+S (Windows) saves the schema
- **Schema format** — stored as a JSON object where keys are field slugs and values are objects with `type`, `label`, and type-specific options (e.g. `options` for select, `sub_fields` for repeater, `conditions` for conditional logic)

### Schema JSON format

```json
{
  "title": { "type": "text", "label": "Title" },
  "body": { "type": "richtext", "label": "Body" },
  "category": { "type": "select", "label": "Category", "options": "news,event,product" },
  "featured_image": { "type": "image", "label": "Featured Image" },
  "skills": {
    "type": "repeater",
    "label": "Skills",
    "sub_fields": {
      "skill": { "type": "text", "label": "Skill Name" },
      "level": { "type": "select", "label": "Level", "options": "beginner,intermediate,advanced" }
    }
  },
  "related_posts": {
    "type": "relationship",
    "label": "Related Posts",
    "collection": "post",
    "multiple": true,
    "max": 5
  },
  "event_date": {
    "type": "date",
    "label": "Event Date",
    "conditions": [
      { "field": "category", "operator": "==", "value": "event" }
    ]
  }
}
```

## Scheduling

Set a future publish date on any collection item. Outpost uses lazy publishing — no cron required.

When an item is saved with `status = 'scheduled'` and a `scheduled_at` timestamp, it is invisible to visitors. On every incoming page request, Outpost promotes any scheduled items whose `scheduled_at` is in the past to `published`.

To use: open item → set Status to "Scheduled" → pick date/time → Save.

**Status transitions:**

| From | To | How |
|---|---|---|
| draft | published | Manual in admin |
| draft | pending_review | Editor publishes on require_review collection |
| draft | scheduled | Set Scheduled + pick date |
| pending_review | published | Admin approves |
| pending_review | draft | Admin rejects |
| scheduled | published | Automatic on next page request, or manual |
| scheduled | draft | Manual cancel |
| published | draft | Manual unpublish |

**Time zones:** Scheduled times are stored as UTC. Set time zone in `config.php`:
```php
date_default_timezone_set('America/New_York');
```

**For low-traffic sites** add a cron job to trigger the lazy check:
```
*/5 * * * * curl -s https://yourdomain.com/ > /dev/null 2>&1
```

## Editorial Workflow

Collections can require review before publishing via the `require_review` toggle in the collection schema editor.

**How it works:**
- When `require_review` is enabled on a collection, editors who click "Publish" will have their item set to `pending_review` instead of `published`
- Admins and super_admins can always publish directly, even on require_review collections
- Items in `pending_review` are not visible to site visitors (treated same as drafts)
- Admins approve or reject pending items via the collection items list (bulk or individual)
- Approving sets the item to `published` with `reviewed_by` and `reviewed_at` timestamps
- Rejecting returns the item to `draft` status

**API endpoints:**
- `PUT items/approve` — approve items (admin+ only), body: `{ ids: [1,2,3] }`
- `PUT items/reject` — reject items (admin+ only), body: `{ ids: [1,2,3] }`
- `PUT items/bulk-schedule` — schedule items, body: `{ ids: [1,2,3], scheduled_at: "2026-03-15T09:00:00" }`
- `GET items/labels-with-counts?collection=slug` — returns all folders for a collection with labels and per-label item counts, plus `total_items` and `unfiled_count`
- `POST items/bulk-labels` — bulk add/remove label assignments, body: `{ item_ids: [1,2,3], label_id: 5, action: "add"|"remove" }`
- `GET items?collection=slug&label_id=N` — filter items by label ID; use `label_id=unfiled` for items with no label assignments
- `GET calendar?start=YYYY-MM-DD&end=YYYY-MM-DD&collection=slug` — items within date range for editorial calendar

**Webhook events:** `entry.submitted_for_review`, `entry.approved`, `entry.rejected`

**Database columns added:**
- `collections.require_review` (INTEGER DEFAULT 0)
- `collection_items.reviewed_by` (INTEGER, user ID)
- `collection_items.reviewed_at` (TEXT, ISO timestamp)

## Folders

> Renamed in v1.0.0-beta.7: "Taxonomies" → **Folders**, "Terms" → **Labels**. Old syntax (`<outpost-each taxonomy="slug">`) and old API endpoints (`action=taxonomies`, `action=terms`) still work for backward compatibility.

Create in Admin → Folders. Associate with a collection. Add labels. Types: `flat` (peer labels) or `hierarchical` (parent/child).

```html
<outpost-each folder="categories">
  <a href="/blog?category=<span data-outpost="slug"></span>"><span data-outpost="name"></span></a>
</outpost-each>

<outpost-each collection="post" filteredby:category>
  ...
</outpost-each>
```

Access item's labels inside a loop or single: `item.labels.category` (array).

## Global Fields

Site-wide. Admin → Globals. Use `data-outpost="@field_name"` in templates. Auto-scaffolded from templates on first load. Conditional rendering: `<outpost-if field="@field_name" exists>`.

Storage: key-value pairs in the `settings` table. Saving globals clears all HTML caches.

## Media

Upload files through Admin → Media or via image field pickers in page/item editors.

**Supported types:** jpg, jpeg, png, gif, webp, avif, svg, pdf, mp4, webm, mp3, wav

**Processing (raster images only):**
- Images wider than 2400px are scaled down to 2400px (aspect ratio preserved)
- JPEG and PNG auto-converted to WebP (configurable, enabled by default)
- Thumbnail generated at max 400×300px with `thumb_` prefix
- Pixel dimensions stored in database
- Filenames sanitized + Unix timestamp prefix added

**WebP auto-conversion:**
- Converts JPEG and PNG uploads to WebP using GD (25–50% smaller)
- Skips GIF (animation), SVG (vector), WebP (already optimized), AVIF, PDF, video
- Preserves PNG alpha/transparency
- Original file replaced on disk with `.webp` extension
- Disable: `define('OUTPOST_WEBP_AUTO_CONVERT', false);`
- Quality: `define('OUTPOST_WEBP_QUALITY', 85);` (0–100, default 85)
- Existing files are not retroactively converted

**Focal point:**
- Every image has a focal point (default: 50% 50% center)
- Set by clicking on the image preview in the media detail panel
- Stored as `focal_x` and `focal_y` (0–100) in the database
- Template filter: `data-outpost="field" data-type="focal"` outputs `X% Y%` for CSS
- Use with `object-position` or `background-position`:
```html
<img src="<img data-outpost="hero_image" data-type="image">" style="object-fit: cover; object-position: data-outpost="hero_image" data-type="focal";">
```
- Works on collection items: `<span data-outpost="featured_image" data-type="focal"></span>`
- Returns `50% 50%` if no custom focal point set

**Media folders:**
- Organize files into folders (up to 3 levels of nesting)
- **Multi-folder assignment:** Files can belong to multiple folders simultaneously (junction table)
- Each folder has an auto-generated unique slug for use in template tags
- Folder sidebar in media library: All Files, Unfiled, folder tree
- Create, rename, delete folders via right-click context menu
- **Bulk folder creation:** Type comma-separated names to create up to 50 folders at once
- Drag files from grid onto folders to **add** (not move — files keep existing assignments)
- Uploads auto-assigned to the currently active folder
- Deleting a folder: files become unfiled, children re-parented
- **Resizable detail sidebar:** Drag left edge to resize (220–500px), width persisted in localStorage
- **Right-click context menu on files:** Shows folder badges, quick "Add to folder", Copy Path, Delete
- Folders are logical — files stay in `uploads/` on disk, URLs unchanged
- **Role-based folder restrictions:** Editors can be scoped to specific folders via per-user grants (Admin → Users → Edit → Media Folder Access). No grants = full access. Admins/super_admins always have full access. Restricted editors cannot create/rename/delete folders.
- API: `GET/POST/PUT/DELETE api.php?action=media-folders`
- Bulk create: `POST api.php?action=media-folders/bulk` with `{names: [...], parent_id: null}`
- Move/add/remove: `PUT api.php?action=media/move` with `{ids: [...], folder_id: N, action: "set"|"add"|"remove"}`
- Get folders for item: `GET api.php?action=media/folders&media_id=N`
- Assign folders: `PUT api.php?action=media/assign-folders` with `{media_id: N, folder_ids: [...]}`
- Folder grants: `GET/PUT api.php?action=users/media-folder-grants` with `{user_id: N, folder_ids: [...]}`
- Filter media list: `GET api.php?action=media&folder_id=N` (or `unfiled`)

**Gallery from media folders (template tag):**
```html
<outpost-each media_folder="photos">
  <img src="<span data-outpost="url"></span>" alt="<span data-outpost="alt"></span>"
       style="object-position: <span data-outpost="focal_x"></span>% <span data-outpost="focal_y"></span>%;">
</outpost-each>
```
- `photos` is the folder slug (auto-generated, unique, visible via right-click → Copy template tag)
- Available fields: `img.url`, `img.alt` (or `img.alt_text`), `img.width`, `img.height`, `img.focal_x`, `img.focal_y`, `img.mime_type`, `img.filename`
- Images returned in reverse-chronological order (newest first)
- All string fields are HTML-escaped

**Bulk upload:**
- Drop multiple files or select multiple in file picker
- Upload queue drawer (bottom-right) with per-file progress bars
- 2 concurrent uploads at a time
- Shows WebP savings per file
- Cancel individual uploads while in progress
- Auto-collapses 3s after all complete

**Storage path:** `outpost/content/uploads/` (flat, no subdirectories). A symlink at `outpost/uploads` points to `content/uploads/` for URL compatibility.

```
outpost/content/uploads/1700000000_hero-photo.webp        # original (converted)
outpost/content/uploads/thumb_1700000000_hero-photo.webp   # thumbnail
```

URL stored in DB (and output by `| image`): `/outpost/uploads/1700000000_hero-photo.webp` (served via symlink)

**SVG sanitization:** `<script>` tags, `on*` event handlers, and external `xlink:href` references are removed before saving.

**In templates:**
```html
<img src="<img data-outpost="hero_image" data-type="image">" alt="<span data-outpost="hero_title"></span>">
<img src="<img data-outpost="hero_image" data-type="image">" style="object-fit: cover; object-position: data-outpost="hero_image" data-type="focal";">
<img data-outpost="logo" data-scope="global" data-type="image">
<a href="<a data-outpost="brochure_pdf" data-type="link" href="#">Link</a>" download>Download</a>
```

**Max upload size:** 10 MB default. Override in `config.php`:
```php
define('OUTPOST_MAX_UPLOAD_SIZE', 25 * 1024 * 1024);
```

Never delete `outpost/content/uploads/` — not regenerated automatically.

## Members

Built-in member system for front-end user accounts. Separate from admin users.

**Member roles:** `free_member`, `paid_member`

**Member status:** `active` (default), `suspended` (blocked from login). Manage in Admin → Members.

Member pages at `outpost/member-pages/`:
- `login.php` — sign in (shows resend-verification button for unverified members)
- `register.php` — new account (shows "check your email" when verification enabled)
- `forgot-password.php` — request reset email
- `reset-password.php` — set new password via token
- `verify-email.php` — email verification landing page (validates token, marks email verified)
- `logout.php` — sign out
- `profile.php` — view/edit member profile

**Email verification** (optional): Enable in Admin → Settings → Members. When on, new members receive a verification email with a 24-hour token link. They cannot sign in until verified. Members registered before the setting was enabled are grandfathered in. Admins can manually verify from Admin → Members. Setting key: `require_email_verification` ('0'/'1').

## Protecting Pages

### Quick start — checking member authentication

```php
<?php
require_once $_SERVER['DOCUMENT_ROOT'] . '/outpost/members.php';

// Check if ANY member is logged in (returns true/false)
$isLoggedIn = OutpostMember::check();

// Get the full member record (returns array or null)
$member = OutpostMember::current();

if (!$member) {
    header('Location: /outpost/member-pages/login.php?redirect=' . urlencode($_SERVER['REQUEST_URI']));
    exit;
}

// Check specific role
if ($member['role'] === 'paid_member') {
    // Paid member — show premium content
}

// Check if membership has expired
if ($member['member_expires'] && strtotime($member['member_expires']) < time()) {
    // Expired — prompt renewal
}

// Available member fields:
// $member['id'], $member['email'], $member['display_name'],
// $member['role'], $member['status'], $member['member_expires'],
// $member['created_at']
?>
```

The member system works out of the box. No configuration is needed beyond having Outpost installed. The `members.php` file handles sessions, cookie validation, and database lookups automatically. Just `require_once` it and call the static methods.

### Page-level visibility

Every page in Admin → Pages has a visibility setting:

| Setting | Who can access |
|---|---|
| `public` | Anyone (default) |
| `members` | Any logged-in member |
| `paid` | `paid_member` role only |

Enforced by `front-router.php` and `index.php` before template renders. Non-public pages redirect to login with `?redirect=` param.

### Template-level protection (PHP partial)

```php
<?php
// partials/member-gate.php
require_once $_SERVER['DOCUMENT_ROOT'] . '/outpost/members.php';
if (!OutpostMember::check()) {
    header('Location: /outpost/member-pages/login.php?redirect=' . urlencode($_SERVER['REQUEST_URI']));
    exit;
}
?>
```

Include in template:
```html
<outpost-include partial="member-gate" />
<!-- rest of template renders only for logged-in members -->
```

Paid-only gate:
```php
<?php
require_once $_SERVER['DOCUMENT_ROOT'] . '/outpost/members.php';
$member = OutpostMember::current();
if (!$member) {
    header('Location: /outpost/member-pages/login.php?redirect=' . urlencode($_SERVER['REQUEST_URI']));
    exit;
}
if ($member['role'] !== 'paid_member') {
    header('Location: /upgrade');
    exit;
}
?>
```

### OutpostMember methods

```php
OutpostMember::check()           // bool — is a member logged in?
OutpostMember::current()         // array|null — full member record or null
// member fields: id, email, display_name, role, member_expires, status
```

## Admin Roles

Admin users have one of four roles (separate from front-end member roles):

| Role | Description |
|---|---|
| `super_admin` | Full access + user management + site settings + destructive ops. Reserved for site owner. |
| `admin` | Full content + code editor + user management. Cannot change site-level settings. |
| `developer` | Full content + code editor + site files. No user/member management or settings. |
| `editor` | Content only (pages, collections, media, globals, navigation, folders, form submissions). No code, analytics, channels, form builder, settings, or user management. Can be restricted to specific collections. Cannot edit locked pages. |

### Collection-Scoped Editors

Editors can be restricted to specific collections by an admin:

1. Go to Admin → Users → [Editor User]
2. Under "Collection Access", check the collections the editor should access
3. If no boxes checked = unrestricted access (backward compatible)

When restricted: only granted collections appear in sidebar, API returns 403 for non-granted collection operations (list, create, update, delete items, bulk ops).

Grants stored in `user_collection_grants` table. API: `GET/PUT users/grants?user_id=N`.

### Per-Page Locks

Admins can lock individual pages to prevent editing by non-admin roles:

- `super_admin` and `admin` can lock/unlock pages and always edit locked pages
- `developer` and `editor` see a "locked" banner and cannot edit or delete locked pages (API returns 403)
- Lock set via "Lock page" checkbox in Page sidebar tab
- Locked pages show lock icon in pages list
- `locked` field only settable by admin+; silently stripped for other roles

## Password Reset

### Admin users

1. "Forgot password?" on login page → `POST /outpost/api.php?action=auth/forgot` with `{ email }`
2. API generates `bin2hex(random_bytes(32))` token, stores with 1-hour expiry, emails reset link
3. Reset URL: `/outpost/?reset_token=TOKEN`
4. Login SPA detects `?reset_token=` on mount, shows reset view, strips token from URL via `history.replaceState`
5. `POST /outpost/api.php?action=auth/reset` with `{ token, password }` → token NULLed, password updated

### Members

1. `forgot-password.php` → `POST /outpost/member-api.php?action=forgot`
2. Reset URL: `/outpost/member-pages/reset-password.php?token=TOKEN`
3. `reset-password.php` validates token on load, shows form, updates on POST

Security: tokens are 64-char hex, 1-hour expiry, single-use, endpoint always returns 200 (no email enumeration).

## Forms

### Visual Form Builder

Create forms visually in Admin → Forms. The builder provides 14 field types: text, email, phone, url, number, textarea, select, radio, checkbox, date, time, hidden, html, section.

Render a builder form in any template:

```html
<outpost-form slug="contact" />
```

This outputs a complete `<form>` with proper field types, labels, validation, honeypot spam protection, and submit button. No JavaScript required. Outpost injects no default form styles — your site CSS has full control.

**Rendered HTML structure** (example with name, email, message fields):

```html
<form method="POST" action="/outpost/form.php" class="outpost-form outpost-form--contact">
  <input type="hidden" name="_form" value="contact">
  <input type="hidden" name="_redirect" value="/contact?sent=1">
  <input type="hidden" name="_form_id" value="3">
  <!-- honeypot (hidden from real users) -->
  <div style="position:absolute;left:-9999px" aria-hidden="true">
    <input type="text" name="_hp_website" tabindex="-1" autocomplete="off">
  </div>
  <div class="outpost-field outpost-field--text">
    <label for="outpost-f-name">Name <span class="outpost-required">*</span></label>
    <input type="text" id="outpost-f-name" name="name" required placeholder="Your name">
  </div>
  <div class="outpost-field outpost-field--email">
    <label for="outpost-f-email">Email <span class="outpost-required">*</span></label>
    <input type="email" id="outpost-f-email" name="email" required>
  </div>
  <div class="outpost-field outpost-field--textarea">
    <label for="outpost-f-message">Message</label>
    <textarea id="outpost-f-message" name="message" rows="5"></textarea>
    <p class="outpost-field-desc">We typically respond within 24 hours.</p>
  </div>
  <button type="submit" class="outpost-form-submit">Send Message</button>
</form>
```

**Field settings:** label, machine name, required, placeholder, description, default value, CSS classes. Choice fields (select/radio/checkbox) support `Label|value` pairs. Number fields support min/max/step.

**Form settings:** submit button label, honeypot toggle, confirmation type (message or redirect URL).

**Submissions:** View in Admin → Submissions. Star, add notes, bulk actions (mark read, star, archive, delete), filter by form and status.

### Manual HTML Forms

You can also hand-code forms. Point form action at `/outpost/form.php`. Use hidden `_form` and `_redirect` fields:

```html
<form action="/outpost/form.php" method="POST">
  <input type="hidden" name="_form" value="contact">
  <input type="hidden" name="_redirect" value="/contact?sent=1">
  <input type="hidden" name="_notify" value="you@example.com"> <!-- optional override -->
  <input type="text" name="name" required>
  <input type="email" name="email" required>
  <textarea name="message"></textarea>
  <button type="submit">Send</button>
</form>
```

reCAPTCHA: add a hidden `g-recaptcha-response` field — `form.php` validates it automatically if a secret key is configured in settings.

**URL pre-population:** Form fields auto-fill from query parameters (e.g. `?name=John` pre-fills the name field).

### CSS Classes

Builder forms use `outpost-form`, `outpost-field`, `outpost-field--{type}`, `outpost-form-submit`. Style with:

```css
.outpost-form { /* form wrapper */ }
.outpost-field { /* field wrapper */ }
.outpost-field label { /* field label */ }
.outpost-field input, .outpost-field textarea, .outpost-field select { /* inputs */ }
.outpost-required { /* required asterisk */ }
.outpost-field-desc { /* help text */ }
.outpost-form-submit { /* submit button */ }
```

## Channels (External Data Sources)

Channels connect Outpost to external data sources — REST APIs, RSS feeds, and CSV files. If Collections are content you create in the admin, Channels are content that flows in from somewhere else. No plugins, no Composer, no build step — connect to any JSON API, RSS/Atom feed, or CSV file, cache data in SQLite, and render it in templates with the same template syntax.

### Concept

- **Collections** = content you create and edit in the admin panel
- **Channels** = content that flows in from external sources (read-only in Outpost)
- Three source types: **REST API**, **RSS Feed** (RSS 2.0 + Atom 1.0), **CSV** (any URL serving CSV data)
- All three types use identical template syntax: `<outpost-each channel="slug">` — templates are type-agnostic
- Both collections and channels support URL patterns for single-item pages
- Channel data is cached in SQLite with configurable TTL
- Type is chosen at creation and cannot be changed afterward

### Creating a Channel

Navigate to Admin → Channels → New Channel. Choose the source type (REST API / RSS Feed / CSV), then the 4-step builder wizard:

1. **Connect** — choose source type, enter URL, configure type-specific options (see below), test connection
2. **Schema** — view discovered fields, set data path (API only), pick fields to include, set ID and slug fields
3. **Configure** — name, slug, cache TTL (5min/15min/1hr/6hr/daily/manual), max items, sort field, URL pattern
4. **Preview** — see cached items in table, get auto-generated template code

**REST API** connect options: URL, HTTP method, auth method (None/API Key/Bearer Token/Basic Auth), custom headers, query parameters.

**RSS Feed** connect options: Feed URL, optional auth. Auto-detects RSS 2.0 vs Atom 1.0. Fields auto-mapped (see RSS Field Mapping below).

**CSV** connect options: CSV URL, delimiter (comma/tab/semicolon/pipe), "first row is headers" toggle. Headers auto-sanitized to lowercase with underscores.

### Authentication Types

| Type | How it works |
|---|---|
| None | No authentication headers sent |
| API Key | Custom header name + value (e.g. `X-API-Key: abc123`) |
| Bearer Token | `Authorization: Bearer {token}` header |
| Basic Auth | `Authorization: Basic {base64(user:pass)}` header |

### Config JSON Structure

The `config` column stores all connection settings as JSON:

```json
{
  "url": "https://api.example.com/v1/listings",
  "method": "GET",
  "auth_type": "bearer",
  "auth_config": { "bearer_token": "sk_live_..." },
  "headers": { "Accept": "application/json" },
  "params": { "status": "active", "limit": "100" },
  "data_path": "data.listings",
  "id_field": "id",
  "slug_field": "slug",
  "pagination": {
    "type": "offset",
    "page_param": "page",
    "limit_param": "limit",
    "per_page": 50,
    "cursor_param": "after",
    "cursor_path": "meta.next_cursor"
  }
}
```

### RSS Field Mapping

RSS channels auto-map standard feed fields. Both RSS 2.0 and Atom 1.0 are supported:

| Field | RSS 2.0 source | Atom 1.0 source |
|---|---|---|
| `title` | `<title>` | `<title>` |
| `link` | `<link>` | `<link rel="alternate" href="...">` |
| `description` | `<description>` | `<summary>` |
| `pubDate` | `<pubDate>` | `<published>` or `<updated>` |
| `author` | `<author>` or `<dc:creator>` | `<author><name>` |
| `content` | `<content:encoded>` | `<content>` |
| `category` | `<category>` (comma-joined) | `<category term="...">` |
| `guid` | `<guid>` | `<id>` |
| `enclosure_url` | `<enclosure url="...">` | — |

Default ID field: `guid`. Default slug source: `title`.

### CSV Field Mapping

CSV channels use column headers as field names. Headers are sanitized: lowercase, spaces replaced with underscores, special characters removed. If "first row is headers" is disabled, columns are named `col_0`, `col_1`, etc.

Supported delimiters: comma (default), tab, semicolon, pipe. UTF-8 BOM is stripped automatically.

**Google Sheets tip:** Use File → Share → Publish to web → CSV. The URL looks like `https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/.../pub?output=csv`.

### Data Path Extraction (REST API only)

Dot-notation to target the array in the API response:
- `data.listings` → `response['data']['listings']`
- `results` → `response['results']`
- `data.items.records` → `response['data']['items']['records']`
- Empty string → response root (when API returns a plain array)

### Channel Loops

```html
<outpost-each channel="listings">
  <h2><span data-outpost="title"></span></h2>
  <p><span data-outpost="description"></span></p>
  <span><span data-outpost="price"></span></span>
<outpost-else>
  <p>No listings available.</p>
</outpost-each>
```

Loop options (space-separated after channel name):

| Option | Example | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `limit:N` | `limit:5` | Show at most N items |
| `orderby:field` | `orderby:price` | Sort by field (descending) |

Combined: `<outpost-each channel="listings" limit:6 orderby:updated_at>`

### Single Channel Items

```html
<outpost-single channel="listings">
  <h1><span data-outpost="title"></span></h1>
  <p><span data-outpost="description"></span></p>
<outpost-else>
  <p>Listing not found.</p>
</outpost-single>
```

Fetches by `?slug=` (set automatically by URL pattern routing, same as collections).

### Channel URL Patterns

Set a URL pattern in the Configure step (e.g. `/listing/{slug}`). The front router:
1. Queries all active channels with a `url_pattern` set
2. Matches the request URI against each pattern
3. Extracts the slug from the URL
4. Sets `$_GET['slug']` for use by `<outpost-single>`
5. Renders `{channel-slug}.html` template, falls back to `channel.html`

### Caching and Sync

- Channel data is cached in the `channel_items` SQLite table (all three types)
- Each channel has a configurable TTL (5min, 15min, 1hr, 6hr, daily, manual-only)
- **Auto-sync**: When a template renders a channel loop and the TTL has expired, Outpost syncs automatically before rendering
- **Manual sync**: Click "Sync Now" in Admin → Channels → [channel] → Sync tab
- **Sync log**: Every sync is logged with item counts (added/updated/removed), duration, and errors
- If the external source is down, the last cached data is served

### Pagination Support (REST API only)

REST API channels support two pagination modes for APIs that return paginated data (RSS and CSV channels always return all items in a single fetch):

- **Offset-based**: Uses `page` and `limit` query params. Outpost increments the page until no more data returns.
- **Cursor-based**: Uses a cursor param (e.g. `after`) with the cursor value extracted from the response via a dot-notation path (e.g. `meta.next_cursor`).

### Channel vs Collection Comparison

| Feature | Collections | Channels |
|---|---|---|
| Data source | Admin editor | External REST API, RSS feed, or CSV file |
| Template loop | `<outpost-each collection="slug">` | `<outpost-each channel="slug">` |
| Single item | `<outpost-single collection="slug">` | `<outpost-single channel="slug">` |
| URL patterns | `/post/{slug}` | `/listing/{slug}` |
| Editable in admin | Yes | No (read-only cache) |
| Storage | `collection_items` table | `channel_items` table (cache) |
| Loop options | `limit`, `orderby`, `paginate`, `filteredby`, `related` | `limit`, `orderby` |

### Mixing Channels and Collections

```html
<!-- Latest blog posts from your CMS -->
<outpost-each collection="post" limit:3 orderby:published_at>
  <a href="<span data-outpost="url"></span>"><span data-outpost="title"></span></a>
</outpost-each>

<!-- Latest property listings from external MLS API -->
<outpost-each channel="mls" limit:6>
  <div class="listing-card"><span data-outpost="address"></span> — <span data-outpost="price"></span></div>
</outpost-each>
```

### Channel Admin API Endpoints

| Action | Method | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `channels` | GET | List all channels with item counts |
| `channels&id=X` | GET | Get single channel with full config |
| `channels` | POST | Create channel (type: api, rss, or csv — immutable) |
| `channels&id=X` | PUT | Update channel |
| `channels&id=X` | DELETE | Delete channel (cascades items + log) |
| `channels/sync&id=X` | POST | Trigger manual sync |
| `channels/sync-log&id=X` | GET | Get sync history (last 50) |
| `channels/discover` | POST | Schema discovery — dispatches by type (API/RSS/CSV) |
| `channels/items&id=X` | GET | Preview cached items (paginated) |

### Channel Database Tables

**`channels`:** `id`, `slug` (UNIQUE), `name`, `type` (default 'api'), `config` (JSON), `field_map` (JSON), `cache_ttl` (seconds), `url_pattern`, `sort_field`, `sort_direction`, `max_items`, `status` ('active'|'paused'), `last_sync_at`, `last_error`, `created_at`, `updated_at`

**`channel_items`:** `id`, `channel_id` (FK → channels.id CASCADE), `external_id`, `slug`, `data` (JSON), `sort_value`, `created_at`, `updated_at`. Indexes: `(channel_id)`, `(channel_id, slug)`, `(channel_id, sort_value)`

**`channel_sync_log`:** `id`, `channel_id` (FK → channels.id CASCADE), `status`, `items_synced`, `items_added`, `items_updated`, `items_removed`, `duration_ms`, `error_message`, `synced_at`

## Sitemap (v4.11.0)

Auto-generated at `/sitemap.xml`. No configuration needed. Includes all CMS pages and published collection items.

Each entry includes:
- `<loc>` — full URL (base URL auto-detected from HTTP request or `OUTPOST_SITE_URL`)
- `<lastmod>` — ISO 8601 date of last update
- `<changefreq>` — homepage: daily, pages: weekly, collection items: monthly
- `<priority>` — homepage: 1.0, pages: 0.8, collection items: 0.6

**Excluded automatically:** Draft pages, member-only pages (`visibility = 'members'`), paid-only pages (`visibility = 'paid'`), admin paths (`/outpost/`).

**Per-collection control:** Each collection has a `sitemap_enabled` setting (default: on). Toggle off in collection settings to exclude that collection's items from the sitemap.

**Auto robots.txt:** Outpost auto-generates `/robots.txt` with:
```
User-agent: *
Allow: /
Disallow: /outpost/
```
