```
Sitemap: https://yourdomain.com/sitemap.xml
```

## RSS Feeds (v4.11.0)

Auto-generated RSS 2.0 feeds for any collection. Visitors can subscribe via any feed reader (Feedly, NetNewsWire, Inoreader, etc.).

### Feed URLs

| URL | Description |
|-----|-------------|
| `/feed.xml` | Site-wide feed (latest 50 items from all feed-enabled collections) |
| `/feed`, `/rss`, `/rss.xml` | Aliases for the site-wide feed |
| `/{collection-slug}/feed` | Per-collection feed (e.g., `/post/feed`) |
| `/{collection-slug}/feed.xml` | Per-collection feed with `.xml` extension |

### Enabling Feeds

Each collection has a `feed_enabled` setting (default: off). Toggle on in collection settings to include that collection in feeds. The site-wide feed only aggregates items from feed-enabled collections.

### Feed Content

Each `<item>` includes:
- `<title>` — item title (falls back to slug)
- `<link>` and `<guid>` — full URL from collection URL pattern
- `<pubDate>` — RFC 2822 date of publication
- `<description>` — excerpt or first 500 chars of body (HTML stripped, truncated at word boundary)
- `<dc:creator>` — author field (if present)

Feed metadata: RSS 2.0 with `atom:link` self-reference, `dc:` namespace, generator = "Outpost CMS".

### Auto-Discovery

When `<outpost-seo>` or `<outpost-meta>` is in your template, Outpost automatically injects `<link rel="alternate" type="application/rss+xml">` tags in `<head>` for:
- The site-wide feed (`/feed.xml`)
- Each feed-enabled collection's individual feed

No manual template changes needed.

## Site Search (v4.11.0)

Full-text site-wide search across all pages and collection items. Drop a tag, get search.

### Template Tag

```html
<!-- Basic search widget -->
<outpost-search placeholder="Search the site..." />

<!-- Restrict to a specific collection -->
<outpost-search collection="post" limit="10" />
```

Attributes:
- `placeholder` — input placeholder text (default: "Search…")
- `collection` — optional, restrict results to one collection slug
- `limit` — max results (default: 20, max: 50)

### Custom HTML

For full control over the search markup, use data attributes:

```html
<div data-outpost-search data-limit="20">
  <input type="search" data-outpost-search-input placeholder="Search...">
  <div data-outpost-search-results></div>
</div>
```

The JS client auto-discovers `[data-outpost-search]` containers and wires up behavior.

### Search API

`GET api.php?action=search/site&q=keyword&limit=20&offset=0`

Public endpoint, no auth required. Rate limited: 30 requests per minute per IP.

Response:
```json
{
  "results": [
    {
      "type": "collection_item",
      "id": 42,
      "collection_slug": "post",
      "title": "My Blog Post",
      "url": "/post/my-blog-post",
      "image": "/uploads/hero.jpg",
      "excerpt": "...text with <mark>matching</mark> words..."
    }
  ],
  "total": 15,
  "query": "keyword"
}
```

### Search Index

- Table: `search_index` — stores title, content_text (HTML stripped), URL, image for every published page and collection item
- **Auto-reindex:** If index is empty when a query arrives, rebuilds automatically
- **Incremental updates:** `search_index_page()` and `search_index_item()` called on save
- **Manual rebuild:** `POST api.php?action=search/reindex` (admin auth required)
- **Relevance:** Title matches ranked higher than body matches

### Search Analytics

Every search query is logged to `analytics_searches` (same table used by the Analytics dashboard's Search tab). Tracks query text, result count, and session ID.

### URL State

Queries reflected in `?q=` URL parameter. Shareable: visiting a URL with `?q=keyword` auto-runs the search on page load.

### Client Assets

- **JS:** `/outpost/search-client.js` — 134 lines, vanilla JS, no dependencies. Debounced input (300ms), Enter to search, loading/empty/error states.
- **CSS:** `/outpost/search-client.css` — 78 lines, minimal styles for auto-generated content only. Override with your own CSS targeting `.outpost-search-result`, `.outpost-search-result-title`, `.outpost-search-result-excerpt mark`, etc.

Both are auto-injected when `data-outpost-search` is detected in the rendered page.

## Analytics

Built-in tracker injected before `</body>` on every page. Fires GET to `/outpost/track.php`. Bot detection via user-agent. No cookies, no fingerprinting, all data in SQLite.

### Analytics Tabs

The Analytics page has 6 tabs:

1. **Traffic** — pageviews, unique visitors, top pages, referrers, device breakdown, geo data (if enabled)
2. **Events** — custom events tracked via `outpost.track(name, properties)`
3. **Goals** — conversion tracking with configurable goals
4. **Search** — internal site search analytics (v2.3)
5. **Content** — content performance cohorts by page publish date (v2.3)
6. **Funnels** — member lifecycle funnel: Visit → Sign Up → Login → Upgrade (v2.3, only visible when members enabled)

### Search Analytics (v2.3)

Track what visitors search for on your site. Two methods:

**JavaScript API** (call from your search results page):
```javascript
// Track a search query and the number of results
outpost.trackSearch('keyword', 5);

// Track when a user clicks a search result
outpost.trackSearchClick('keyword', '/path/clicked');
```

**Automatic detection**: The tracker automatically detects search queries from URL params `?q=`, `?search=`, `?s=` and tracks them (results count defaults to 0 for auto-detected searches).

**API endpoint**: `GET api.php?action=dashboard/search&period=30days`
Returns: `total_searches`, `unique_queries`, `click_rate`, `top_queries`, `zero_result_queries`, `top_clicked`, `chart` (daily trend).

Database table: `analytics_searches` (query, results_count, clicked_path, session_id, created_at).

### Content Performance Cohorts (v2.3)

Groups pages by publish date into 5 cohorts: Last 7 days, Last 30 days, Last 90 days, Last 6 months, Older. Joins with `analytics_hits` to show traffic distribution per cohort.

**API endpoint**: `GET api.php?action=dashboard/cohorts&period=30days`
Returns: Array of cohorts, each with `page_count`, `total_views`, `avg_views_per_page`, `view_share`, `top_pages`.

No new database tables — computed from existing `pages` + `analytics_hits`.

### Goal Funnels (v2.3)

Tracks member lifecycle as a 4-stage funnel:
1. **Visitors** — unique sessions from `analytics_hits`
2. **Sign Ups** — `member_events` where `event_type = 'signup'`
3. **Logins** — distinct users with `event_type = 'login'`
4. **Upgrades** — `event_type = 'role_change'` (role changes to paid tiers)

Events are recorded automatically at registration, login, and role change — no developer action needed.

**API endpoint**: `GET api.php?action=dashboard/funnels&period=30days`
Returns: `stages` array (count + conversion rate per stage), `recent_events` (last 20 member events).

Database table: `member_events` (user_id, event_type, details, session_id, created_at).

### Geo Enrichment (v2.3)

Optional country-level visitor analytics. Double-gated for privacy:
1. Upload a MaxMind GeoLite2-Country `.mmdb` file to `content/data/`
2. Enable via Settings → Advanced → Geo Analytics toggle

When enabled, `track.php` looks up visitor IPs against the mmdb file and stores only the 2-letter country code (e.g., `US`, `DE`, `GB`) in `analytics_hits.country_code`. Raw IPs are never stored.

**Pure-PHP MMDB reader** (`mmdb-reader.php`): ~270 LOC, zero Composer dependencies, supports IPv4 and IPv6.

**API endpoints**:
- `GET api.php?action=dashboard/geo&period=30days` — country breakdown with counts and percentages
- `GET api.php?action=dashboard/geo/status` — check mmdb file status (exists, size, modified date)
- `POST api.php?action=dashboard/geo/upload` — upload mmdb file (multipart form, field name: `mmdb`)
- `DELETE api.php?action=dashboard/geo/delete` — remove mmdb file and disable setting

GA4 integration (optional):
```html
<outpost-if field="@ga4_id" exists>
<script async src="https://www.googletagmanager.com/gtag/js?id=<span data-outpost="ga4_id" data-scope="global"></span>"></script>
<script>
  window.dataLayer = window.dataLayer || [];
  function gtag(){dataLayer.push(arguments);}
  gtag('js', new Date());
  gtag('config', '<span data-outpost="ga4_id" data-scope="global"></span>');
</script>
</outpost-if>
```

## SEO — `<outpost-seo />` tag

Place `<outpost-seo />` once in `head.html`. It outputs a complete SEO block automatically for every page type. No parameters, no configuration, no manual OG tags needed in templates.

```html
<!-- partials/head.html -->
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8">
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
  <outpost-seo />
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="/assets/style.css">
</head>
<body>
```

**What it outputs by page type:**

- **Static page** (`/about`): `<title>`, `<meta name="description">`, `<link rel="canonical">`, `og:type=website`, `og:site_name`, `og:title`, `og:description`, `og:image`, `og:url`, `twitter:card`, `twitter:title`, `twitter:description`, `twitter:image`, `twitter:site`
- **Collection single** (`/post/slug`): All of the above with `og:type=article`, `article:published_time`, `article:author`, plus JSON-LD `Article` schema block
- **Paginated list** (`/blog?page=2`): Adds `<link rel="prev">` and `<link rel="next">`
- **Members/paid page**: Adds `<meta name="robots" content="noindex">`

**Fallback chain:**

| Tag | Priority |
|---|---|
| `<title>` | Page Meta Title → item `title` → page `title` → `@site_name` |
| `meta description` | Page Meta Description → item `excerpt` → first 30 words of `body` → `@site_tagline` |
| `og:image` / `twitter:image` | Page OG Image → item `featured_image` → `@og_image` |
| `og:title` | Same as title, without ` — Site Name` suffix |
| `canonical` | `OUTPOST_SITE_URL` + current path (omitted if constant not set) |
| `og:type` | `article` on single pages, `website` elsewhere |
| `article:author` | Item `author` field → `@author_name` |
| `twitter:site` | `@twitter_handle` global (include `@` prefix) |

**Title format:** `{Page Title} — {Site Name}`. Homepage: Meta Title if set, otherwise just site name.

**Required global fields** (auto-registered on first use, set in Admin → Globals):
- `@og_image` — default Open Graph image (1200×630px recommended)
- `@twitter_handle` — Twitter/X handle with `@` prefix, e.g. `@mysite`
- `@site_tagline` — fallback meta description for pages without one set

**Required config:** `define('OUTPOST_SITE_URL', 'https://example.com');` in `config.php` for canonical URLs.

**JSON-LD Article schema** (collection singles only):
```json
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "Article",
  "headline": "Item title",
  "description": "Resolved description",
  "image": "https://example.com/outpost/uploads/featured.jpg",
  "datePublished": "2024-02-01T08:00:00+00:00",
  "dateModified": "2024-02-10T12:00:00+00:00",
  "url": "https://example.com/post/my-article",
  "author": { "@type": "Person", "name": "Author Name" }
}
```

`<outpost-seo />` replaces `<span data-outpost="title"></span>` and `<span data-outpost="description"></span>`. Do not use both — `<outpost-seo />` already outputs `<title>` and description. Individual meta tags still work in legacy sites using v1 syntax.

## WordPress Import

Migrate posts from WordPress using the native WXR XML export.

1. In WordPress: Tools → Export → Posts → Download Export File
2. In Outpost: Admin → Import → upload `.xml` file → select target collection → configure options → Import

**What gets imported:** title, body content (sanitized HTML), excerpt, slug, publish date, status, categories/tags (as folder labels if matched).

**Import options:**
- Posts to import: "Published only" or "All" (includes drafts)
- On duplicate: "Skip" (keep existing) or "Overwrite" (replace by slug match)

Images in post content still point to old WordPress domain after import — update manually.

## Code Editor

In-admin site file editor at Admin → Code. Requires `developer` or higher role. Edits files at `OUTPOST_SITE_ROOT`, excluding the `outpost/` directory.

Features: syntax highlighting (HTML, CSS, JS), tab bar with dirty-state indicator, file tree with create/rename/delete, find-in-files search, Outpost template autocomplete inside `data-outpost` and `<outpost-*>` elements.

v2 syntax highlighting (v4.1.1): High-contrast, colorblind-accessible decorations for all Outpost v2 template patterns:
- `data-outpost="field"` — bold gold/amber
- `data-type="richtext"` / `data-bind="attr:field"` — bold cyan/teal
- `data-scope="global"` — bold magenta/pink
- `<outpost-each>`, `<outpost-single>`, `<outpost-if>`, etc. — bold green
- `<!-- outpost:blockname -->` — amber background
- `<!-- outpost-settings: ... -->` — blue background
Both dark and light mode variants. Legacy v1 syntax (`{{ }}`, `{% %}`, `{# #}`) highlighting also supported for backwards compatibility.

v2 autocomplete (v4.1.1): Context-aware completions triggered by:
- Typing `data-` — suggests `data-outpost`, `data-type`, `data-scope`, `data-bind` with all type values
- Typing `<outpost-` — suggests all custom elements with collection-specific loops
- Typing `<!-- outpost` — suggests block comment and settings comment patterns

Endpoints added to `code-editor.php`:
- `code/files` GET — list site code files
- `code/read` GET — read a site file
- `code/write` POST — write a site file
- `code/create` POST — create file or folder
- `code/rename` POST — rename/move (validates extension)
- `code/delete` DELETE — recursive for directories
- `code/search` GET `?q=term` — full-text search, max 200 results
- `code/context` GET — returns globals, collections, schema, forms, menus, folders (used by autocomplete and Forge)
- `code/assets` GET `?theme=slug` — list all files in a theme's `assets/` directory (CSS, JS, images, fonts). Returns `{ files: [{ name, path, size }] }`

## Forge (Visual Tag Builder)

Built into the Code Editor. Select HTML in any `.html`/`.htm` file, right-click to open the Forge context menu, or press Cmd+E / Ctrl+E to jump straight to Make Editable. Right-clicking without a selection shows "Insert Asset" and "Insert Component" options.

Forge uses smart detection — it analyzes the selected HTML and reorders the context menu so the most likely action appears first. It also pre-selects the field type in the Make Editable form.

### Smart Forge AI (v4.5.0)

When an AI API key is configured (Claude, OpenAI, or Gemini via Settings > Ranger), the "Smart Forge" button in the Code Editor toolbar becomes "Smart Forge AI". Instead of the PHP regex-based scanner, it sends the entire HTML file to the AI with a detailed annotation prompt. The AI:

- Wraps distinct sections in `<!-- outpost:sectionname -->` / `<!-- /outpost:sectionname -->` comments
- Adds `data-outpost="blockname_fieldname"` to every editable element (headings, paragraphs, images, links, buttons, badges, etc.)
- Adds `data-type="richtext"`, `data-type="image"`, `data-type="link"` as appropriate
- Adds `<!-- outpost-settings: -->` for visual properties (bg_color, layout, show_section toggle)
- Wraps nav in `<!-- outpost:nav global -->` and footer in `<!-- outpost:footer global -->`
- Replaces `<title>` and `<meta name="description">` with `<outpost-meta />`

If no AI key is configured, Smart Forge falls back to the existing PHP scanner seamlessly.

**API endpoints:**
- `GET api.php?action=forge/ai-status` — Returns `{ "available": true/false, "provider": "claude"|"openai"|"gemini" }`
- `POST api.php?action=forge/ai-scan` — Body: `{ "html": "..." }`. Returns `{ "template": "annotated HTML", "provider": "claude" }`

**Provider selection:** Uses the default Ranger provider if it has a key configured, otherwise falls back to whichever provider has a key. Models: Claude Sonnet, GPT-4o, Gemini 2.0 Flash.

### Detection rules (priority order)

- `<img>`, `<picture>`, `<figure>`, `src="...jpg"` → Make Editable (image)
- `<a href>` → Make Editable (link)
- `<h1>`–`<h6>` → Make Editable (text)
- Short plain text (<100 chars) → Make Editable (text)
- Long plain text (≥100 chars) → Make Editable (textarea)
- HTML with child elements → Make Editable (richtext)
- `<ul>`/`<ol>` with 2+ links → Menu Loop
- `<nav>`, `<header>`, `<footer>` when matching partial exists → **Use Partial: name** (instant include)
- `<nav>` with links → Extract Partial (with menu option)
- `<header>`, `<footer>` → Extract Partial
- `<title>`, `<meta name="description">` → Meta Tag
- `<form>` → Form
- Multiple repeated sibling elements → Collection Loop
- `<section>`, `<article>`, large `<div>` → Conditional

### Seven actions

**Make Editable** — Wraps selection in an output tag. Fields: name (auto-slugified), type (text, richtext, image, link, textarea, select, color, number, date, toggle), scope (page/global), editable on front-end (appends `data-edit` for on-page editing), use as default (wrapping default — ON by default for all types including image and link). Output: `data-outpost="field"`, `data-outpost="field" data-type="edit"`, `data-outpost="field" data-type="image" data-edit`, `data-outpost="global" data-scope="global" data-edit`, or `data-outpost="field" data-editDefault`.

**Collection Loop** — Wraps selection in `<outpost-each collection="slug">...</outpost-each>`. Fields: collection (dropdown), item variable, limit, order by. **Field mapper**: auto-detects images, headings, paragraphs, and links in selected HTML and maps them to collection fields with auto-guessing.

**Menu Loop** — Wraps navigation links in `<outpost-each menu="slug">...</outpost-each>`. Fields: menu (dropdown of admin menus or free-text slug), link variable. When 2+ links are detected, offers to auto-replace static hrefs and text with `<span data-outpost="url"></span>` and `<span data-outpost="label"></span>`.

**Conditional** — Wraps selection in `<outpost-if field="expression" exists>...</outpost-if>`. Fields: expression, operator (truthy/==/!=), value.

**Extract Partial** — Creates `partials/{name}.html` with the selected content, replaces selection with `<outpost-include partial="name" />`, refreshes file tree, opens the new file in a tab. **Smart nav detection**: when extracting a `<nav>` with 2+ links, offers "Connect to admin menu" checkbox to wrap links in a menu loop automatically. **Apply to all pages**: after extraction, Forge scans other theme files for the same content and offers to replace it in all matching pages at once. **Use Existing Partial**: if a partial with a matching name already exists (e.g. `partials/nav.html` for a `<nav>` selection), "Use Partial: nav" appears at the top of the context menu for instant replacement.

**Meta Tag** — Wraps selection in `<span data-outpost="title"></span>...` or `<span data-outpost="description"></span>...`.

**Form** — Inserts `<outpost-form slug="slug" />`. Three modes: (A) **Auto-create from HTML** — when a `<form>` element is selected, Forge parses the HTML to detect fields (text, email, phone, URL, number, date, time, hidden, textarea, select, radio, checkbox), extracts labels, groups radio/checkbox by name, derives form name, and creates the form in the database. (B) Pick from existing forms dropdown. (C) Manual slug entry.

### Forge Playground

Supports demo HTML sites at the site root — pure HTML/CSS pages that can be annotated with Outpost template tags via Forge site analysis.

### Insert Asset

Right-click in an HTML file (even without selecting text) to see "Insert Asset" in the Forge context menu. Opens a searchable modal that auto-discovers files from the site root `assets/` directory — CSS, JS, images, fonts, and more. Click any file to insert the correct HTML reference tag at the cursor position:

- `.css` → `<link rel="stylesheet" href="/assets/{path}">`
- `.js` → `<script src="/assets/{path}"></script>`
- images → `<img src="/assets/{path}" alt="">`

Powered by the `code/assets` API endpoint which scans the site root `assets/` directory recursively.

### Forge Site Analysis

The `forge/analyze` API endpoint scans all `.html` files at `OUTPOST_SITE_ROOT` and returns annotated versions with Outpost template tags. The `forge/analyze/apply` endpoint writes the annotated files to disk and triggers a template re-scan.

### Preview Tabs

Single-clicking a file opens it as a preview tab (italic name). Clicking another file replaces the preview. Double-click or editing pins the tab. Files opened from Find in Files, command palette, or newly created files are always pinned.

### Keyboard shortcut

Cmd+E / Ctrl+E opens Make Editable directly with the field type pre-selected by smart detection. Status bar shows "Cmd+E to tag" hint when a selection exists in an HTML file.

## Settings

Stored as key-value pairs in `settings` table. Edit in Admin → Settings.

Key settings: site name, SMTP host/port/user/pass, from name/email, form notification email, builder sync key.

Read in PHP: `OutpostDB::setting('key', 'default')`

## Configuration (config.php)

All runtime configuration is set via PHP constants in `outpost/config.php`. No `.env`, no YAML.

```php
<?php
// Minimal required config
define('OUTPOST_SITE_URL', 'https://mysite.com');  // no trailing slash
define('OUTPOST_SYNC_KEY', 'your-random-64-char-key');
```

### Full constant reference

| Constant | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `OUTPOST_SITE_ROOT` | `dirname(OUTPOST_DIR) . '/'` | Site root directory — where HTML templates, partials, and assets live |
| `OUTPOST_CONTENT_DIR` | `content/` | User data directory containing data, uploads, backups |
| `OUTPOST_DB_PATH` | `content/data/cms.db` | SQLite database path |
| `OUTPOST_SITE_URL` | auto-detected | Canonical URL for sitemap, emails, absolute links |
| `OUTPOST_DEBUG` | `false` | Show PHP errors inline — never true in production |
| `OUTPOST_MAX_UPLOAD_SIZE` | `10485760` (10 MB) | Max upload size in bytes |
| `OUTPOST_WEBP_AUTO_CONVERT` | `true` | Auto-convert JPEG/PNG uploads to WebP |
| `OUTPOST_WEBP_QUALITY` | `85` | WebP compression quality (0–100) |
| `OUTPOST_UPLOADS_DIR` | `content/uploads/` | Media upload directory (symlinked at `outpost/uploads`) |
| `OUTPOST_BACKUPS_DIR` | `content/backups/` | Backup zip storage directory |
| `OUTPOST_CACHE_DIR` | `cache/` | Cache directory (not under content/ — regenerated) |
| `OUTPOST_CACHE_TTL` | `3600` | HTML page cache TTL in seconds |
| `OUTPOST_TEMPLATE_CACHE` | `true` | Enable compiled template cache (set `false` during dev) |
| `OUTPOST_SESSION_NAME` | `outpost_session` | Admin session cookie name |
| `OUTPOST_MEMBER_SESSION` | `outpost_member` | Member session cookie name |
| `OUTPOST_SESSION_LIFETIME` | `86400` (24h) | Admin session lifetime in seconds |
| `OUTPOST_MEMBER_LIFETIME` | `2592000` (30 days) | Member "remember me" lifetime in seconds |
| `OUTPOST_SYNC_KEY` | (none) | Builder sync API secret key |
| `OUTPOST_SMTP_HOST` | (none) | SMTP hostname |
| `OUTPOST_SMTP_PORT` | `587` | SMTP port (587=STARTTLS, 465=SSL, 25=plain) |
| `OUTPOST_SMTP_USER` | (none) | SMTP username |
| `OUTPOST_SMTP_PASS` | (none) | SMTP password |

## REST API

Base URL: `/outpost/api.php?action=ENDPOINT`

All mutating requests require `X-CSRF-Token` header (token received at login). Session-based auth via cookies.

### Public endpoints (no auth)

| Action | Method | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `auth/forgot` | POST | Send password reset email |
| `auth/reset` | POST | Set new password via token |

### Admin endpoints (require auth)

| Action | Methods | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `auth/login` | POST | Sign in |
| `auth/logout` | POST | Sign out |
| `auth/me` | GET | Current user (includes `update_available` + `latest_version` for admin/super_admin) |
| `pages` | GET, PUT | List and update pages |
| `fields/bulk` | PUT | Bulk page field update |
| `globals` | GET | Global fields |
| `collections` | GET, POST, PUT, DELETE | Collections CRUD |
| `items` | GET, POST, PUT, DELETE | Collection items CRUD (see create example below) |
| `items/inline` | PUT | Partial field update for collection items (on-page editing) |
| `items/bulk-status` | PUT | Bulk update item status (draft/published/pending_review) |
| `items/bulk-delete` | DELETE | Bulk delete items |
| `items/bulk-schedule` | PUT | Bulk schedule items to a specific date/time |
| `items/approve` | PUT | Approve pending_review items (admin+ only) |
| `items/reject` | PUT | Reject pending_review items back to draft (admin+ only) |
| `items/preview-token` | POST | Generate preview token for draft/scheduled items |
| `items/labels-with-counts` | GET | Folders + labels with per-label item counts for a collection |
| `items/bulk-labels` | POST | Bulk add/remove label assignments for items |
| `calendar` | GET | Items by date range for editorial calendar (params: start, end, collection) |
| `folders` | GET, POST, PUT, DELETE | Folders CRUD |
| `labels` | GET, POST, PUT, DELETE | Folder labels CRUD |
| `item-labels` | GET, PUT | Label assignments |
| `media` | GET, PUT, DELETE | Media library (GET: ?folder_id= filter; PUT updates alt_text, focal_x, focal_y, folder_id) |
| `media/upload` | POST | Upload file (optional folder_id form field) |
| `media/transform` | POST | Resize or crop a raster image (GD required) |
| `media/move` | PUT | Move/add/remove media files to/from folders (ids[], folder_id, action: set/add/remove) |
| `media/folders` | GET | Get folder IDs for a media item (media_id param) |
| `media/assign-folders` | PUT | Set all folder assignments for a media item (media_id, folder_ids[]) |
| `media-folders` | GET, POST, PUT, DELETE | Media folder CRUD (max 3 levels deep, auto-generated slugs) |
| `media-folders/bulk` | POST | Create multiple folders at once (names[], parent_id, max 50) |
| `users` | GET, POST, PUT, DELETE | Admin users CRUD |
| `users/grants` | GET, PUT | Collection grants for editor users (GET returns granted IDs, PUT replaces all grants) |
| `users/media-folder-grants` | GET, PUT | Media folder grants for editor users (folder_ids[]) |
| `editor/media-lookup` | GET | Look up media record by path (?path=), returns alt_text and metadata |
| `editor/media-alt` | POST | Update media alt_text by path ({path, alt_text}) |
| `members` | GET, PUT, DELETE | Member management |
| `menus` | GET, POST, PUT, DELETE | Navigation menus CRUD |
| `loops` | GET, POST, DELETE | Saved query loops |
| `settings` | GET, PUT | Site settings |
| `site/detect` | GET | Detect site structure at OUTPOST_SITE_ROOT |
| `forge/analyze` | POST | Scan site HTML and return annotated templates |
| `forge/analyze/apply` | POST | Apply annotated templates to disk |
| `forms` | GET | List form names with counts |
| `forms/submissions` | GET, PUT, DELETE | Form submissions (supports `form`, `form_id`, `status`, `starred` filters) |
| `forms/submissions/star` | PUT | Toggle star on submission |
| `forms/submissions/status` | PUT | Change submission status (active/archived/trashed) |
| `forms/submissions/notes` | PUT | Update submission notes |
| `forms/submissions/bulk` | POST | Bulk actions: read, star, unstar, archive, delete, restore |
| `forms/config` | GET, PUT | Form notification settings |
| `forms/test-smtp` | POST | Send SMTP test email |
| `forms/export` | GET | Export submissions as CSV |
| `forms/builder` | GET, POST, PUT, DELETE | Form builder CRUD (create/read/update/delete form definitions) |
| `forms/builder/duplicate` | POST | Duplicate a form |
| `code/files` | GET | List site code files at OUTPOST_SITE_ROOT |
| `code/read` | GET | Read a site file |
| `code/write` | POST | Write a site file |
| `code/create` | POST | Create file or folder |
| `code/rename` | POST | Rename/move file |
| `code/delete` | DELETE | Delete file or directory |
| `code/search` | GET | Full-text search in site files |
| `code/context` | GET | Globals + collections for autocomplete |
| `code/assets` | GET | List site asset files (CSS, JS, images) |
| `cache/clear` | POST | Clear HTML + template cache |
| `backup/create` | POST | Create backup zip (DB + uploads) |
| `backup/list` | GET | List available backups |
| `backup/download` | GET | Download a backup zip |
| `backup/restore` | POST | Restore from uploaded backup zip (super_admin only) |
| `backup/delete` | DELETE | Delete a backup file |
| `backup/settings` | GET, PUT | Auto-backup settings (enabled, frequency, max retention) |
| `dashboard/stats` | GET | Site stats snapshot |
| `dashboard/analytics` | GET | Full analytics data |
| `dashboard/search` | GET | Search analytics (top queries, zero-results, click-through) |
| `dashboard/cohorts` | GET | Content performance cohorts by page age |
| `dashboard/funnels` | GET | Member lifecycle funnel (visitors → signups → logins → upgrades) |
| `dashboard/geo` | GET | Country breakdown from geo-enriched pageviews |
| `dashboard/geo/status` | GET | Geo mmdb file status (exists, size, modified) |
| `dashboard/geo/upload` | POST | Upload MaxMind GeoLite2-Country .mmdb file |
| `dashboard/geo/delete` | DELETE | Remove mmdb file and disable geo |
| `dashboard/activity` | GET | Recent activity log |
| `dashboard/seo` | GET | SEO health data |
| `dashboard/content` | GET | Content overview |
| `dashboard/members` | GET | Member stats |
| `content/schema` | GET | Public schema (read-only) |
| `content/pages` | GET | Public pages (read-only) |
| `content/collections` | GET | Public collections (read-only) |
| `content/items` | GET | Public collection items (read-only) |
| `content/folders` | GET | Public folders (read-only, optional ?collection= filter) |
| `content/labels` | GET | Public folder labels (read-only) |
| `content/menus` | GET | Public navigation menus (read-only, ?slug= for single) |
| `content/media` | GET | Public media listing (read-only) |
| `content/syntax` | GET | Template syntax reference (read-only) |
| `import/wordpress` | POST | Import WordPress WXR export |
| `sync/key` | GET, POST | Builder sync API key |
| `sync/key/regenerate` | POST | Regenerate sync API key |
| `channels` | GET, POST, PUT, DELETE | Channels CRUD (external data sources) |
| `channels/sync` | POST | Trigger channel sync |
| `channels/sync-log` | GET | Channel sync history |
| `channels/discover` | POST | Schema discovery (fetch URL, return JSON schema) |
| `channels/items` | GET | Preview cached channel items |
| `revisions` | GET | List revisions for entity (entity_type, entity_id params) |
| `revisions/restore` | POST | Restore a revision (snapshots current state first) |

### Creating a collection item — full example

```js
// Step 1: Get CSRF token (required for all POST/PUT/DELETE)
const meRes = await fetch('/outpost/api.php?action=auth/me', {
  credentials: 'include',
});
const { csrf_token } = await meRes.json();

// Step 2: Create the item
const res = await fetch('/outpost/api.php?action=items', {
  method: 'POST',
  credentials: 'include',
  headers: {
    'Content-Type': 'application/json',
    'X-CSRF-Token': csrf_token,
  },
  body: JSON.stringify({
    collection_id: 3,
    slug: 'my-first-post',
    status: 'published',
    data: {
      title: 'My First Post',
      body: '<p>Hello world.</p>',
      featured_image: '/outpost/uploads/hero.jpg'
    }
  })
});

// Step 3: Handle response
if (!res.ok) {
  const err = await res.json();
  // err.error contains the message, e.g. "Slug already exists"
  console.error('Create failed:', err.error);
} else {
  const item = await res.json();
  console.log('Created item:', item.id);
}
```

**Required fields:** `collection_id` (numeric ID), `slug` (URL-safe, unique within collection). **Optional:** `data` (field values object), `status` (`draft`/`published`/`scheduled`), `scheduled_at` (ISO datetime for scheduled status).

**Error responses:** 401 = no active session, 403 = invalid CSRF token, 400 = validation error (missing required field or duplicate slug).

**API key alternative:** Use `Authorization: Bearer op_...` header instead of session + CSRF. No CSRF token needed with API keys.

### Member API

Base URL: `/outpost/member-api.php?action=ENDPOINT`

**Session-based endpoints** (use cookies):

| Action | Method | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `login` | POST | Member sign in (sets session cookie) |
| `logout` | POST | Member sign out |
| `register` | POST | New member registration (auto-login via session) |
| `forgot` | POST | Send reset email (public) |
| `reset` | POST | Reset password via token (public) |
| `resend-verify` | POST | Resend verification email (public, 60s rate limit) |
| `me` | GET | Current member (accepts session or bearer token) |
| `profile` | PUT | Update member profile (accepts session or bearer token) |
| `password` | PUT | Change member password (accepts session or bearer token) |

**JWT bearer token endpoints** (for mobile apps / headless clients):

| Action | Method | Auth | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `token` | POST | None | Exchange email+password for a JWT bearer token. Rate limit: 10/min. |
| `token/register` | POST | None | Register new member and receive JWT. Rate limit: 5/5min. |
| `token/refresh` | POST | Bearer | Exchange valid JWT for a fresh one (30-day expiry). |
| `token/me` | GET | Bearer | Get member profile via bearer token. |

**JWT details:**
- Algorithm: HS256 (HMAC-SHA256), pure PHP — no external libraries
- Token lifetime: 30 days for members
- Secret: derived from database path (unique per installation)
- Header: `Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIs...`
- CSRF tokens are NOT required for bearer-authenticated requests

**Token response format:**
```json
{
  "token": "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9...",
  "member": { "id": 50, "name": "Jane", "email": "jane@example.com", "tier": "free" },
  "expires_at": "2026-04-19T12:00:00+00:00"
}
```

### CORS Configuration

By default, cross-origin requests are only allowed from `localhost`. For mobile apps:

Set the `api_cors_origins` setting in the settings table:
- `*` — allow all origins (recommended for mobile apps)
- `https://app.example.com, https://www.example.com` — allow specific origins
- (not set) — localhost only

This setting applies to `api.php`, `member-api.php`, and `content-api.php`.

```sql
INSERT INTO settings (key, value) VALUES ('api_cors_origins', '*')
  ON CONFLICT(key) DO UPDATE SET value = excluded.value;
```

## Public Content API

Read-only, unauthenticated JSON API. No API key, no session cookie, no Authorization header needed. CORS open to all origins (`Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *`). Only published content returned. Members-only and paid pages excluded.

Base URL: `/outpost/api.php?action=content/ENDPOINT`

Rate limit: 120 requests per 60 seconds per IP. Exceeds → HTTP 429 with `Retry-After` header.

All responses wrap payload in `data`. List endpoints include `meta.total`, `meta.limit`, `meta.offset`. Errors return `{ "error": "message" }` with appropriate HTTP status code.

### Pages vs Items — choosing the right endpoint

This is the most common source of confusion. Outpost has two content types:

- **Pages** (`content/pages`) — standalone pages (Home, About, Contact). Identified by URL **path** (e.g. `/about`). Returns all pages if no `path` param. No pagination (typically <20 pages).
- **Items** (`content/items`) — entries in a **collection** (blog posts, products, team members). Identified by **collection slug + item slug**. Supports pagination, sorting, and folder filtering.

Rule of thumb: fixed URL like `/about`? Use `content/pages&path=/about`. One of many entries in a list? Use `content/items&collection=blog&slug=my-post`.

### Schema
```
GET api.php?action=content/schema
```
Returns all collections (with field definitions), pages with fields, all folders.

### Pages
```
GET api.php?action=content/pages
GET api.php?action=content/pages&path=/about
```
Returns public pages with `fields` as flat key-value map. Members-only and paid pages excluded.

### Items
```
GET api.php?action=content/items&collection=blog
GET api.php?action=content/items&collection=blog&slug=my-post
```

Query params: `limit` (default 10, max 100), `offset`, `orderby` (also accepts `orderBy`; values: created_at|updated_at|published_at|slug|sort_order), `order` (ASC|DESC), `{folder-slug}=label-slug` for filtering.

Response item fields: `id`, `slug`, `title`, `url`, `status`, `created_at`, `updated_at`, `published_at`, `fields{}` (all field values), `labels{}` (labels grouped by folder slug). The `title` is pulled from the item's title field (fallback: slug). The `url` is built from the collection's `url_pattern` with slug substituted (e.g. `/post/{slug}` → `/post/my-first-post`).

### Folders / Labels
```
GET api.php?action=content/folders
GET api.php?action=content/folders&collection=blog
GET api.php?action=content/labels&folder=category
```
The `?collection=` parameter on folders filters to folders belonging to that collection only.

### Menus
```
GET api.php?action=content/menus
GET api.php?action=content/menus&slug=main
```
Without `?slug=`: list all menus (slug, name, item_count). With `?slug=`: single menu with full item tree including nested `children` arrays. Each item has `label`, `url`, `target`, `children[]`.

### Media
```
GET api.php?action=content/media
GET api.php?action=content/media&limit=20&offset=0
```

### Globals
```
GET api.php?action=content/globals
```
Returns all global fields as a flat key-value object.

JavaScript examples:
```js
// Fetch blog listing with pagination
const res = await fetch('/outpost/api.php?action=content/items&collection=blog&limit=10&orderby=published_at&order=DESC');
const { data: posts, meta } = await res.json();
// meta.total = total items, meta.limit = per page, meta.offset = current offset
// each post has: post.title, post.url, post.fields.*, post.labels.*

// Filter by folder label
const res = await fetch('/outpost/api.php?action=content/items&collection=blog&category=tutorials');

// Single post by slug
const res = await fetch('/outpost/api.php?action=content/items&collection=blog&slug=my-post');
const { data: post } = await res.json();
// post.title, post.url, post.fields.body (HTML), post.labels.category (array)

// Fetch a navigation menu
const res = await fetch('/outpost/api.php?action=content/menus&slug=main');
const { data: menu } = await res.json();
// menu.items[].label, menu.items[].url, menu.items[].children[]

// Fetch globals
const res = await fetch('/outpost/api.php?action=content/globals');
const { data: globals } = await res.json();
// globals.site_name, globals.author_name, etc.

// Error handling
const res = await fetch('/outpost/api.php?action=content/items&collection=nonexistent');
if (!res.ok) {
  // 404 if collection doesn't exist, 429 if rate limited
  const err = await res.json();
  console.error(err.error);
}
```

**Content API always returns published items only.** Draft, scheduled, and pending_review items are excluded. Members-only pages are excluded from the pages endpoint. Empty collections return `{ "data": [], "meta": { "total": 0 } }` — not an error.

### HTTP status codes

| Status | Meaning | When |
|---|---|---|
| `200` | OK | Request succeeded |
| `400` | Bad Request | Missing required param (e.g. `collection` for items) |
| `404` | Not Found | Collection/page/item/menu slug doesn't exist |
| `405` | Method Not Allowed | Used POST/PUT/DELETE instead of GET |
| `429` | Too Many Requests | Rate limit exceeded; check `Retry-After` header |

Error response: `{ "error": "Human-readable message" }`

### Error handling pattern
```js
async function outpost(action, params = {}) {
  const url = new URL('https://yoursite.com/outpost/api.php');
  url.searchParams.set('action', action);
  Object.entries(params).forEach(([k, v]) => url.searchParams.set(k, v));
  const res = await fetch(url.toString());
  if (!res.ok) {
    const body = await res.json().catch(() => ({}));
    if (res.status === 404) return null;
    if (res.status === 429) throw new Error(`Rate limited — retry after ${res.headers.get('Retry-After')}s`);
    throw new Error(body.error || `HTTP ${res.status}`);
  }
  return res.json();
}
// Returns null for 404, throws for other errors
const page = await outpost('content/pages', { path: '/about' });
```

### Edge cases

- Empty collection → `{ "data": [], "meta": { "total": 0 } }` (not an error)
- Non-existent folder filter label → empty results (not an error)
- Members-only/paid pages → silently excluded from `content/pages`
- Draft/scheduled items → silently excluded from `content/items`
- `limit` over 100 → silently capped at 100
- `offset` past total → returns empty `data: []` with correct `meta.total`
- Globals page (`__global__`) → never in `content/pages`; use `content/globals`
- No menus defined → `content/menus` returns `{ "data": [], "meta": { "total": 0 } }`

## GraphQL API

Full GraphQL API at `/outpost/graphql.php`. Single endpoint, POST with JSON body `{"query": "...", "variables": {}}`. Zero external dependencies — pure PHP parser.

**Authentication:**
- Queries (reads) — no auth required, same as REST Content API
- Mutations (writes) — require `Authorization: Bearer <api_key>` header (same API keys as admin API)

**Interactive explorer:** `/outpost/graphql-playground.html` loads GraphiQL. Add `#key=op_xxx` to URL for mutation auth.

### Query types
```graphql
{
  collections { slug name itemCount fields { name type } }
  collection(slug: "blog") { name fields { name type } itemCount }
  items(collection: "blog", limit: 10, offset: 0, orderBy: "published_at", order: "DESC", folder: "categories", label: "tutorials") {
    data { id slug title url status createdAt updatedAt publishedAt fields labels }
    total limit offset
  }
  item(collection: "blog", slug: "my-post") { title url fields labels }
  pages { id path title metaTitle metaDescription fields }
  page(path: "/") { title fields }
  globals  # Returns JSON object of all global fields
  menus { slug name itemCount }
  menu(slug: "main") { name items { label url target children { label url } } }
  media(limit: 50, offset: 0) { data { id filename path mimeType width height altText } total }
  folders(collection: "blog") { slug name type collectionSlug labelCount }
  labels(folder: "categories") { id slug name description parentId itemCount data }
  schema { collections { slug fields { name type } } pages { path title } globals { name type } folders { slug } }
}
```

### Mutation types (require Bearer auth)
```graphql
mutation {
  createItem(collection: "blog", slug: "new-post", status: "published", data: {title: "Hello", body: "<p>World</p>"}) { id slug url }
  updateItem(id: 42, data: {title: "Updated"}, status: "published") { id slug updatedAt }
  deleteItem(id: 42)
  updatePage(id: 1, fields: {hero_title: "Welcome"}, metaTitle: "Home") { id path fields }
  updateGlobals(fields: {site_name: "My Site"})
  deleteMedia(id: 7)
  assignLabels(itemId: 42, labelIds: [1, 2])
  removeLabels(itemId: 42, labelIds: [3])
  createCollection(name: "Products", slug: "product", schema: {fields: [{name: "title", type: "text"}]}) { id slug }
  updateCollection(id: 5, name: "Updated", urlPattern: "/shop/{slug}") { id slug }
  createFolder(collectionId: 1, name: "Tags", slug: "tags") { slug name }
  createLabel(folderId: 1, name: "Tutorials", slug: "tutorials") { id slug name }
}
```

### Features
- Field selection — only requested fields are returned
- Aliases — `blogs: items(collection: "blog") { total }`
- Variables — `query($slug: String!) { item(collection: "blog", slug: $slug) { ... } }`
- Fragments — `...PostFields` spread syntax
- Full introspection — `__schema`, `__type` (works with GraphiQL/Apollo Studio)
- Partial updates — `updateItem` merges provided data into existing data JSON
- Rate limiting — 120 requests/60s per IP

### Response format
```json
// Success
{"data": {"collections": [...]}}

// Error
{"errors": [{"message": "Collection 'xyz' not found"}]}
```

## Sync API

Machine-to-machine API for the Outpost Builder desktop app to sync site files. Authenticated with a static API key in `X-Outpost-Key` header. HTTPS enforced on non-localhost.

Base URL: `/outpost/sync-api.php?action=ENDPOINT`

Rate limiting: 5 failed auth attempts per hour per IP → 429 with `Retry-After` header.

File constraints: max 2 MB per file, allowed extensions: `php html htm css js json svg txt md yml yaml xml png jpg jpeg gif webp ico woff woff2 ttf otf eot`, paths must stay within `OUTPOST_SITE_ROOT` and outside `outpost/`.

### Endpoints

| Action | Method | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `pull` | GET | Full site snapshot: all site files (base64), media list, DB snapshot + raw DB file |
| `push` | POST | Deploy site files to server; auto-creates backup before writing; clears cache after |
| `backup/list` | GET | List all server-side backups (newest first, auto-prunes >30 days old) |
| `backup/restore` | POST | Restore a backup by ID; auto-creates backup of current state first |

### Pull response structure
```json
{
  "outpost_version": "5.0.0",
  "site_url": "https://example.com/outpost",
  "pulled_at": "2024-03-01T10:00:00+00:00",
  "site_files": {
    "index.html": "<base64>",
    "about.html": "<base64>",
    "partials/head.html": "<base64>",
    "assets/style.css": "<base64>"
  },
  "uploads": [{ "path": "uploads/photo.jpg", "url": "...", "size": 204800 }],
  "db_snapshot": { "settings": {}, "pages": [], "collections": [], ... },
  "db_file": "<base64-encoded SQLite binary>"
}
```

The `db_snapshot` excludes sync API key and admin credentials.

### Push request body
```json
{
  "files": {
    "index.html": "<base64>",
    "about.html": "<base64>",
    "partials/nav.html": "<base64>",
    "assets/style.css": "<base64>"
  },
  "push_content": false
}
```

File paths are relative to `OUTPOST_SITE_ROOT`. Paths inside `outpost/` are rejected.

`push_content: true` + `db_file` replaces the entire server database — overwrites all live data. Use with caution.

### Builder CLAUDE.md

The Builder desktop app auto-generates a `CLAUDE.md` file containing the site's Sync API key on every pull. This allows Claude Code and other AI agents to interact with the site's API without manual key configuration.

### Backup storage
`outpost/content/backups/{backup-id}/` with `manifest.json` + `files/` directory. Backups older than 30 days auto-pruned on `backup/list`.

## Deploy

Copy the `outpost/` directory to your server. First request initializes the DB.

**Preserve on re-deploy:**
- `outpost/content/` — entire user data directory (database, uploads, backups)
- `outpost/cache/` — safe to clear, not required to preserve

**File permissions:**
```bash
chmod -R 755 outpost/content outpost/cache
```

**Apache .htaccess:**
```apache
RewriteEngine On
RewriteCond %{REQUEST_FILENAME} !-f
RewriteCond %{REQUEST_FILENAME} !-d
RewriteRule ^ index.php [QSA,L]
```

**Nginx:**
```nginx
location / {
    try_files $uri $uri/ /index.php?$query_string;
}
```

**Clear cache after template changes:**
```bash
rm -f outpost/cache/*.html
rm -f outpost/cache/templates/*.php
```

Or use Clear Cache in the admin top bar.

**Upgrading from beta to stable:** Use Settings → Updates (built-in auto-updater) for beta.11+, or manually copy core PHP files + `admin/` directory for older versions. Database migrations run automatically. Themes, data, and uploads are never touched. See the deploy guide for full upgrade instructions.

## Auto-Updater

Check for and apply core updates from Settings → Updates. Requires `super_admin` role.

**Auto-check on login:** The `auth/me` response includes `update_available` (bool) and `latest_version` (string) for admin/super_admin users. Uses the same 5-minute cache — zero extra GitHub calls. A green dot badge appears on Settings in the sidebar when an update is available.

**How it works:**
1. `GET api.php?action=updates/check` — fetches latest GitHub Release, compares against `OUTPOST_VERSION`, cached 5 minutes
2. `POST api.php?action=updates/apply` — downloads release zip, validates URL (must be github.com/tonyshawjr/outpost), validates zip paths (zip slip prevention), extracts core files + updates managed themes

**Files replaced:** root `.php` files, `admin/`, `docs/`, `member-pages/`, `tools/`
**Files never touched:** site root files (HTML templates, partials, assets), `data/`, `uploads/`, `cache/` (cache is cleared post-update)

**Security:** URL validated via `parse_url()` with strict hostname + path check. HTTPS enforced. All zip entry paths validated before extraction. Only `super_admin` can trigger updates.

### Enhanced Template Error Messages

The template engine (v1.9.0+) provides developer-friendly error diagnostics:

**Pre-compilation validation:** `OutpostTemplate::validate()` checks for balanced block tags (`<outpost-if>`, `<outpost-each>`, `<outpost-single>`) before compilation. Reports unclosed tags, mismatched end tags, and unexpected `<outpost-else>` with exact line numbers and file names.

**Source line tracking:** The compiler injects `/* @line:N */` markers into compiled PHP. When a runtime error occurs, `mapCompiledLineToSource()` traces the PHP error line back to the original template source line.

**Friendly error display:** Admin users see a styled error page with:
- The error type and file name
- The exact template line number (when available)
- A source context view showing surrounding lines with the error line highlighted
- Human-readable error messages translated from PHP internals (e.g. "Unknown variable $item" instead of "Undefined variable $item")

Non-admin visitors see a generic "Something went wrong" page with no source details.

**Error translations:** `translateError()` converts common PHP errors:
- `Undefined variable $x` → "Unknown variable $x. Check that it's defined in a <outpost-each> or <outpost-single> block."
- `syntax error` → "Template syntax error. Check for unclosed tags or invalid template expressions."
- `Undefined array key "x"` → "Field "x" is not defined. Check the field name in your template."
- `Call to undefined function x()` → "Unknown function x(). This may indicate a missing Outpost feature or a typo."

## Security

Outpost is hardened against common web vulnerabilities:

**Authentication:** Session-based with CSRF tokens. Bcrypt password hashing (cost 12). Secure cookies in production. TOTP 2FA with replay prevention and single-use tokens.

**Rate limiting:**
- Admin/member login: 5 attempts per 60s per IP + session
- TOTP verify: 5 attempts per 5 minutes per IP
- Forgot/reset password: 5 requests per 5 minutes per IP
- Form submissions: 5 per 60s per IP
- API mutations: 60 per 60s per API key

**SSRF prevention:** All outbound HTTP (channels, webhooks) validated — scheme must be http/https, hostname resolved and checked against private/loopback/link-local/metadata IP ranges. `CURLOPT_PROTOCOLS` restricted.

**File security:** Upload extension + MIME allowlist. SVG sanitized with DOM parser (removes script, foreignObject, on* handlers, dangerous URIs). Path traversal prevention via `realpath()` scoping in code editor, media upload, and page delete.

**HTML sanitization:** Richtext stored clean via allowlist filter. URI schemes restricted to http, https, mailto, relative paths. Blocks `javascript:`, `data:`, `vbscript:`.

**API keys:** Bcrypt-hashed, prefix-based O(1) lookup. Per-key rate limiting and usage tracking.

**CORS:** Strict origin matching — only exact `localhost` or `127.0.0.1` accepted.

**Access control:** 6-role capability system. Page visibility gates (public, members, paid) enforced at router level. Suspended members detected via periodic DB revalidation.

**Build admin SPA (if modifying `src/`):**
```bash
cd cms/
npm install
npm run build   # outputs to php/admin/
```

## Database Migrations

Outpost uses idempotent inline migrations — no migration files or version tracking. Pattern:

```php
$db = OutpostDB::connect();
$cols = array_column($db->query("PRAGMA table_info(users)")->fetchAll(), 'name');
if (!in_array('new_column', $cols)) {
    $db->exec("ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN new_column TEXT");
}
```

Migrations run on first request to the relevant endpoint. Safe to run multiple times. Schema is created and evolved automatically — no manual SQL needed.

## Database Schema

Outpost uses 20 SQLite tables in five groups. Single `.db` file at `outpost/content/data/cms.db` (path set by `OUTPOST_DB_PATH`).

**Content:** `pages`, `fields`, `collections`, `collection_items`
**Channels:** `channels`, `channel_items`, `channel_sync_log`
**Folder & Navigation:** `folders`, `labels`, `item_labels`, `menus`
**Users & Members:** `users`, `members`, `user_collection_grants`, `settings`
**Media, Forms & Logs:** `media`, `forms` (builder definitions), `form_configs`, `form_submissions` (with form_id, status, starred, notes, user_agent), `analytics_hits` (with country_code for geo), `analytics_searches` (query, results_count, clicked_path), `analytics_events`, `member_events` (user_id, event_type, details for funnel tracking), `activity_log`

Key columns:

**`pages`:** `id`, `title`, `slug`, `body`, `meta_title`, `meta_description`, `og_image`, `visibility` (`'public'|'members'|'paid'`), `status` (`'published'|'draft'`), `locked` (0|1 — admin-only lock), `sort_order`, `created_at`, `updated_at`

**`fields`:** `id`, `page_id`, `theme`, `field_name`, `field_type`, `content`, `default_value`, `options` (JSON), `sort_order` — one row per unique (page_id, theme, field_name). The `theme` column is vestigial from pre-v5; always empty string in v5+.

**`collections`:** `id`, `slug`, `name`, `singular_name`, `url_pattern`, `schema` (JSON field definitions), `sort_field`, `sort_direction`, `items_per_page`, `created_at`, `updated_at`. `sort_field` only accepts: `created_at`, `updated_at`, `published_at`, `slug`, `sort_order` — invalid values default to `created_at`.

**Collection schema format:** The admin UI and API expect schemas in flat key-value format:
```json
{
  "title": { "type": "text", "label": "Title" },
  "body": { "type": "richtext", "label": "Body" },
  "featured_image": { "type": "image", "label": "Featured Image" }
}
```
The API auto-normalizes incoming schemas — if you send `{"fields": [{"name": "title", "type": "text"}]}` (array format), it is automatically converted to the flat key-value format above.

Valid field types: `text`, `textarea`, `richtext`, `image`, `link`, `select`, `toggle`, `color`, `number`, `date`, `repeater`, `gallery`, `flexible`, `relationship`.

Repeater sub-fields use the same type set:
```json
{
  "hours": {
    "type": "repeater",
    "label": "Hours",
    "fields": [
      { "name": "day", "type": "select", "options": ["Mon","Tue","Wed","Thu","Fri","Sat","Sun"] },
      { "name": "open", "type": "text" },
      { "name": "close", "type": "text" }
    ]
  }
}
```

**`collection_items`:** `id`, `collection_id`, `slug`, `status` (`'published'|'draft'|'scheduled'|'pending_review'`), `scheduled_at`, `published_at`, `reviewed_by`, `reviewed_at`, `data` (JSON), `created_at`, `updated_at`

**`members`:** `id`, `email`, `password_hash`, `display_name`, `role` (`'free_member'|'paid_member'`), `status` (`'active'|'suspended'`), `member_expires`, `created_at`, `updated_at`

**`user_collection_grants`:** `id`, `user_id` (FK→users), `collection_id` (FK→collections) — M:N grants restricting editor users to specific collections. UNIQUE(user_id, collection_id). No rows = unrestricted access.

**`settings`:** `id`, `key`, `value` — key-value store for site settings and globals

## Cache

Two cache layers:

1. **HTML page cache** — full rendered HTML stored in `outpost/cache/{md5(path+query)}.html`. Keyed on path + sorted query string, so `?category=bricks` and `?page=2` get separate cache files. TTL: `OUTPOST_CACHE_TTL` (default 1 hour). Cleared on content save or via admin. Logged-in admins bypass it.

2. **Template cache** — compiled PHP stored in `outpost/cache/templates/{md5(templatePath)}.php`. Recompiled when template file changes (mtime check). Disable with `OUTPOST_TEMPLATE_CACHE = false` during development.

Cache bypass: `?nocache=1` in URL (development only).

### Performance optimization for high-traffic sites

**HTML page cache** is the single biggest optimization. When enabled (default), fully rendered pages are served as static HTML files — PHP and SQLite are bypassed entirely. TTL defaults to 1 hour. Admins always bypass cache to see fresh content.

**SQLite WAL mode** is enabled by default for concurrent read performance. Outpost sets `PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL` at connection time. No configuration needed.

**Minimize template-level API calls.** Each `<outpost-each collection="slug">` runs a SQL query. Use `limit:N` to cap results. Avoid multiple loops against the same collection — restructure templates to use a single loop where possible.

**CDN / reverse proxy:** Place Cloudflare, Fastly, or Nginx caching in front of the site. Outpost's HTML cache headers respect standard cache-control. Static assets in `assets/` at the site root can be served directly by the web server with long cache headers.

**Template warm-up:** After deploying new site templates or clearing the template cache, the first request for each page compiles the template to PHP. Subsequent requests use the cached PHP file. To pre-warm, `curl` each page after deploy.

**SQLite tuning** (optional, in a custom bootstrap):
```php
$db = OutpostDB::connect();
$db->exec("PRAGMA cache_size = -20000");    // 20 MB page cache
$db->exec("PRAGMA mmap_size = 268435456");  // 256 MB memory-mapped I/O
```

## Architecture — Design Philosophy

Outpost is a **zero-dependency, file-based CMS**. It uses PHP 8, SQLite, and data-attribute HTML templates. There is no Composer, no Node.js runtime, no Redis, no external services.

**Outpost does NOT have:**
- A middleware pipeline
- Action/filter hooks (like WordPress)
- A plugin or extension system
- Event dispatchers or subscribers
- Dependency injection containers

This is a deliberate design decision. Hooks and plugins add architectural complexity, security surface area, and maintenance burden. Outpost keeps the codebase small and auditable instead.

**How to add custom logic:**

1. **Custom API endpoint** — Create a standalone PHP file at your document root. It can query Outpost's SQLite database directly:
```php
<?php
require_once __DIR__ . '/outpost/db.php';
header('Content-Type: application/json');
$items = OutpostDB::fetchAll("SELECT * FROM items WHERE collection_id = ? AND status = 'published'", [1]);
echo json_encode(['items' => $items]);
```

2. **PHP partial in a template** — Create a `.php` file in `partials/` and include it via `<outpost-include partial="name" />`. PHP executes server-side before template output.

3. **Cron job** — For periodic tasks, create a PHP script and run it via cron. Use Outpost's database classes (`OutpostDB`).

4. **JavaScript on the front end** — Call the Public Content API from JS for interactive features.

**Key PHP files:**

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `api.php` | REST API router |
| `engine.php` | Template engine runtime — `cms_text()`, `cms_collection_list()`, etc. |
| `template-engine.php` | Template → PHP compiler |
| `front-router.php` | URL → template file routing |
| `db.php` | SQLite wrapper (`OutpostDB` class) |
| `members.php` | Member authentication (`OutpostMember` class) |
| `config.php` | Constants — paths, limits, version |

**Important:** `engine.php` is overwritten during auto-updates. Never modify it for custom logic — your changes will be lost. Use standalone files instead.

### Intercepting and modifying API requests

Outpost has no built-in middleware pipeline, hooks, or request interceptors. `api.php` processes requests directly — there is no way to register a PHP callback that runs before or after an API action inside Outpost's codebase.

However, you can intercept and modify requests to `api.php` at the **web server level** or with a **PHP proxy wrapper**:

**Approach 1 — PHP proxy wrapper (most flexible):**
Create a PHP file that sits in front of `api.php`, runs your custom logic, then forwards the request:

```php
<?php
// /outpost/api-proxy.php — intercepts requests before api.php
$action = $_GET['action'] ?? '';
$method = $_SERVER['REQUEST_METHOD'];

// PRE-PROCESSING: Log, validate, modify request data
file_put_contents('/tmp/api-log.txt', date('c') . " $method $action\n", FILE_APPEND);

// Example: Block writes from unauthorized IPs
if ($method !== 'GET' && !in_array($_SERVER['REMOTE_ADDR'], ['192.168.1.10'])) {
    http_response_code(403);
    echo json_encode(['error' => 'IP not allowed']);
    exit;
}

// Forward to Outpost
require __DIR__ . '/api.php';
```

**Approach 2 — Output buffering wrapper (modify responses):**
```php
<?php
// /outpost/api-wrapper.php — modifies API responses
ob_start();
require __DIR__ . '/api.php';
$response = ob_get_clean();

$data = json_decode($response, true);
// Modify the response before sending
if (isset($data['pages'])) {
    foreach ($data['pages'] as &$page) {
        $page['word_count'] = str_word_count(strip_tags($page['body'] ?? ''));
    }
}
header('Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8');
echo json_encode($data);
```

**Approach 3 — Apache/Nginx rewrite rules (server-level):**
```apache
# .htaccess — rewrite api.php to your proxy
RewriteEngine On
RewriteCond %{REQUEST_URI} ^/outpost/api\.php
RewriteRule ^outpost/api\.php$ outpost/api-proxy.php [QSA,L]
```

```nginx
# Nginx — rate limit API mutations
limit_req_zone $binary_remote_addr zone=api_writes:10m rate=10r/s;
location = /outpost/api.php {
    limit_except GET {
        limit_req zone=api_writes burst=20 nodelay;
    }
    include fastcgi_params;
    fastcgi_pass unix:/run/php/php-fpm.sock;
    fastcgi_param SCRIPT_FILENAME $document_root$fastcgi_script_name;
}
```

**Approach 4 — Standalone endpoint using Outpost's database:**
```php
<?php
// /my-api.php — custom endpoint with full access to Outpost data
require_once __DIR__ . '/outpost/db.php';
require_once __DIR__ . '/outpost/members.php';
header('Content-Type: application/json');

$member = OutpostMember::current();
if (!$member) { http_response_code(401); echo json_encode(['error' => 'Login required']); exit; }

$items = OutpostDB::fetchAll("SELECT * FROM collection_items WHERE status = 'published' ORDER BY published_at DESC LIMIT 20");
echo json_encode(['items' => $items]);
```

**Which approach to use:**

| Goal | Approach |
|---|---|
| Log/audit API requests | PHP proxy wrapper |
| Add authentication or IP restrictions | Apache/Nginx rules or PHP proxy |
| Modify request data before Outpost processes it | PHP proxy wrapper |
| Modify response data after Outpost processes it | Output buffering wrapper |
| Rate limit API endpoints | Nginx rate limiting |
| Add CORS headers for specific domain | Apache/Nginx headers |
| Build entirely custom API logic using Outpost data | Standalone endpoint |
| Webhook on content changes | PHP proxy that fires webhook after `require api.php` |

**Important:** Proxy files inside `outpost/` are overwritten by auto-updates. Place them at your document root (outside `outpost/`) or use Apache/Nginx configuration.

## Key PHP Classes & Functions

| Symbol | Location | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `OutpostDB::connect()` | `db.php` | Returns PDO connection |
| `OutpostDB::fetchOne($sql, $params)` | `db.php` | Fetch single row |
| `OutpostDB::fetchAll($sql, $params)` | `db.php` | Fetch all rows |
| `OutpostDB::update($table, $data, $where, $params)` | `db.php` | Update helper |
| `OutpostDB::setting($key, $default)` | `db.php` | Read setting value |
| `OutpostAuth::hashPassword($pw)` | `auth.php` | bcrypt hash via `password_hash()` |
| `OutpostMember::check()` | `members.php` | Check member session (returns bool) |
| `OutpostMember::current()` | `members.php` | Full member record or null |
| `OutpostMember::login($id, $pw)` | `members.php` | Member login |
| `OutpostMailer::fromSettings()` | `mailer.php` | Email instance from DB settings |
| `OutpostTemplate::render($file, $themeDir)` | `template-engine.php` | Compile + render template |
| `cms_text($page, $field, $default)` | `engine.php` | Render text field |
| `cms_global($name, $type)` | `engine.php` | Render global field |
| `cms_collection_list($slug, $opts, $cb)` | `engine.php` | Render collection loop |
| `cms_pagination()` | `engine.php` | Render pagination nav |
| `cms_flexible_items($name)` | `engine.php` | Render flexible content loop |
| `cms_relationship_items($name)` | `engine.php` | Render relationship items loop |
| `outpost_cache_path($path)` | `engine.php` | Build cache file path |

## Recipes

### Personal Blog

Collections: `post` with fields `title`, `excerpt`, `featured_image`, `body` (richtext), URL pattern `/post/{slug}`.

```html
<!-- blog.html: list all posts -->
<outpost-include partial="head" />
<outpost-each collection="post" paginate:10 orderby:published_at>
  <article>
    <h2><a href="<span data-outpost="url"></span>"><span data-outpost="title"></span></a></h2>
    <p><p data-outpost="excerpt" data-type="or_body"></p></p>
    <time><span data-outpost="published_at"></span></time>
  </article>
</outpost-each>
<outpost-pagination />
<outpost-include partial="footer" />

<!-- post.html: single post -->
<outpost-include partial="head" />
<outpost-single collection="post">
  <outpost-meta title="" />
  <h1><span data-outpost="title"></span></h1>
  <div class="body"><div data-outpost="body" data-type="richtext"></div></div>
<outpost-else>
  <p>Post not found.</p>
</outpost-single>
<outpost-include partial="footer" />
```

### Category Filtering

```html
<!-- Folder label list -->
<outpost-each folder="category">
  <a href="/blog?category=<span data-outpost="slug"></span>"><span data-outpost="name"></span> (<span data-outpost="count"></span>)</a>
</outpost-each>

<!-- Filtered loop -->
<outpost-each collection="post" paginate:10 filteredby:category>
  <span data-outpost="title"></span>
</outpost-each>
<outpost-pagination />
```

### Dropdown Navigation

```html
<nav>
  <outpost-menu name="main">
    <outpost-if field="children" exists>
      <div class="has-dropdown">
        <a href="<span data-outpost="url"></span>"><span data-outpost="label"></span></a>
        <ul class="dropdown">
          <outpost-each item="children">
            <li><a href="<span data-outpost="url"></span>"><span data-outpost="label"></span></a></li>
          </outpost-each>
        </ul>
      </div>
    <outpost-else>
      <a href="<span data-outpost="url"></span>"><span data-outpost="label"></span></a>
    </outpost-if>
  </outpost-each>
</nav>
```

### Membership Site

```html
<!-- Show teaser to non-members, full content to members -->
<outpost-include partial="member-reveal" />
<!-- partials/member-reveal.php sets $GLOBALS['_outpost_member'] -->

<outpost-if field="@site_name" exists>
<!-- Paywall CTA for non-members rendered via conditional in PHP partial -->
</outpost-if>
```

Page-level: set visibility to "Members Only" or "Paid Members" in the page editor sidebar. No code required.

### Flexible Page Builder

Use flexible content to build modular landing pages:

```html
<!-- landing.html -->
<outpost-include partial="head" />
<outpost-single collection="landing">
  <h1><span data-outpost="title"></span></h1>
  <outpost-each flexible="sections">
    <outpost-if field="layout" equals="hero">
      <section class="hero" style="background-image: url(<span data-outpost="bg_image"></span>)">
        <h2><span data-outpost="heading"></span></h2>
        <p><span data-outpost="text"></span></p>
      </section>
    </outpost-if>
    <outpost-if field="layout" equals="features">
      <section class="features">
        <h2><span data-outpost="heading"></span></h2>
        <div class="body"><div data-outpost="content" data-type="richtext"></div></div>
      </section>
    </outpost-if>
    <outpost-if field="layout" equals="testimonial">
      <blockquote>
        <p><span data-outpost="quote"></span></p>
        <cite><span data-outpost="author"></span></cite>
      </blockquote>
    </outpost-if>
  </outpost-each>
</outpost-single>
<outpost-include partial="footer" />
```

### Related Posts

Use relationship fields to show hand-picked related content:

```html
<outpost-single collection="post">
  <h1><span data-outpost="title"></span></h1>
  <div class="body"><div data-outpost="body" data-type="richtext"></div></div>

  <outpost-each relationship="related_posts">
    <a href="<span data-outpost="url"></span>"><span data-outpost="title"></span></a>
  </outpost-each>
</outpost-single>
```

### Headless / API

```js
// Fetch all published posts
const res = await fetch('/outpost/api.php?action=content/items&collection=post&orderBy=published_at&order=DESC');
const { data: posts, meta } = await res.json();

// Single post
const res = await fetch('/outpost/api.php?action=content/items&collection=post&slug=my-slug');
const { data: post } = await res.json();
const title = post.fields.title;
const html  = post.fields.body;  // rendered HTML

// Filter by category
const res = await fetch('/outpost/api.php?action=content/items&collection=post&category=tutorials');
```

## Setup Wizard & Onboarding (v2.0.0)

### Setup Wizard
Runs automatically on fresh installs. Generates `.htaccess` for Apache URL rewriting. Checks `setup_completed` setting via `auth/me`. Existing sites auto-skip via migration detection (if pages or collections exist, `setup_completed` is set to `1`).

### Content Packs
JSON files in `content-packs/` directory. Manifest: `packs.json` (array of pack defs with id, name, description, icon, themes array). Pack files: `{pack}.json` (e.g. `personal-blog.json`). Each contains: settings, globals, collections (with schema + items + folders), menus. The `blank` pack has no file — just activates the theme. Applied in a single SQLite transaction via `POST setup/apply`.

### Getting Started Checklist
Dashboard card after wizard completion. 5 items: add_logo, edit_homepage, create_post, setup_navigation, configure_brand. Dynamically computed via `GET setup/checklist`. Dismiss via `POST setup/checklist/dismiss` (persists in settings table).

### Contextual Tips
One-line dismissible tips below `.page-header` on admin sections (Pages, Collections, Media, Globals, Navigation, Forms). State in localStorage under `outpost-dismissed-tips`. Component: `ContextualTip.svelte`. Tip definitions in `src/lib/tips.js`.

### EmptyState Component
Reusable `EmptyState.svelte` with props: icon (SVG string), title, description, ctaLabel/ctaAction, secondaryLabel/secondaryAction, searchActive. Used by 11 admin pages for consistent empty states.

### Setup API Endpoints

- `GET setup/packs` — returns available content pack metadata
- `POST setup/apply` — applies wizard choices (site_name, pack) in transaction
- `GET setup/checklist` — returns dynamic checklist completion (add_logo, edit_homepage, create_post, setup_navigation, customize_site)
- `POST setup/checklist/dismiss` — sets `setup_checklist_dismissed = '1'` in settings

## Collaboration & Comments (v3.3.0)

### Team Comments
Leave threaded comments on collection items directly in the editor. The Comments tab in the right sidebar shows all comments with open/resolved filtering, @mention autocomplete, reply threads, and delete.

### @Mentions
Type `@username` in a comment body to mention a team member. Mentions are stored in the `comment_mentions` table for future notification delivery. Email notifications are planned but not yet implemented.

### Client Review Links
Generate shareable URLs for external feedback. Admins create review tokens from Settings > Review Links. Each token can be scoped to a specific page path and given an optional expiry date.

When someone opens `yoursite.com/?review=TOKEN`, a lightweight vanilla JS overlay (<10KB) is injected:
- Floating "Leave Feedback" button in bottom-right corner
- Click any element to open a feedback panel
- Reviewer enters their name (saved in localStorage) and comment
- Submitted comments appear as numbered pins on the page
- Click a pin to see the comment thread

### API Endpoints
- `GET comments` — list comments (params: entity_type, entity_id, page_path, status)
- `POST comments` — create a comment (body: entity_type, entity_id, body, parent_id, page_path)
- `PUT comments?id=X` — update body or status (open/resolved)
- `DELETE comments?id=X` — delete (author or admin only)
- `GET comments/count` — open comment counts by entity
- `GET comments/activity` — recent comments across all content
- `GET review-tokens` — list review tokens (admin only)
- `POST review-tokens` — create a token (body: name, page_path, expires_at)
- `PUT review-tokens?id=X` — toggle active/inactive
- `DELETE review-tokens?id=X` — delete a token
- `POST review/comment` — submit review feedback (public, token auth)
- `GET review/comments` — list review feedback (public, token auth)

### Database Tables
- `comments` — entity_type, entity_id, element_selector, page_path, parent_id, user_id, author_name, author_email, body, status, review_token_id
- `review_tokens` — token, name, page_path, expires_at, active, created_by
- `comment_mentions` — comment_id, user_id, notified

### Ranger Tool
`manage_comments` supports actions: list, create, resolve, delete, activity, create_review_link, list_review_links

## Custom Workflows (v3.2.0)

### What are Workflows?
Workflows let you define custom approval stages for collections. Instead of just Draft and Published, you can create any stages you need — Draft, Copy Review, Design Review, Legal Review, Approved, Published — and control who can move content to each stage.

### Default Workflows
Two workflows are created automatically on first run:
- **Simple** — Draft → Published (the default for all collections)
- **Editorial** — Draft → Copy Review → Approved → Published

### How Workflows Work
1. Create a workflow in the **Workflows** admin page with your custom stages
2. Assign the workflow to a collection in the collection's schema editor
3. When editing items, the status badge shows workflow-aware stage colors
4. Click the status badge to see available transitions based on the current stage and your role
5. Every transition is logged in the History tab with user, timestamp, and optional note

### Stage Configuration
Each stage has:
- **Name** — display name (e.g., "Copy Review")
- **Slug** — machine identifier (e.g., "review")
- **Color** — hex color for status badges and pipeline visualization
- **Can move to** — which other stages this stage can transition to
- **Roles** — which user roles can move content TO this stage

### Required Stages
Every workflow must include `draft` and `published` stages. These cannot be removed.

### Per-Collection Assignment
Each collection can be assigned a different workflow. Collections without a workflow use the default "Simple" workflow (Draft → Published).

### Transition Permissions
Transitions are enforced at two levels:
1. The current stage's `can_move_to` list determines which transitions are possible
2. The target stage's `roles` list determines who can perform the transition

### Transition History
Every stage change creates a `workflow_transitions` record with:
- Item ID, Collection ID
- From stage, To stage
- User ID
- Optional note
- Timestamp

### Webhook Events
The `workflow.transition` webhook event fires on every stage change, including: item_id, collection_id, from_stage, to_stage, user_id, note, and workflow name.

### API Endpoints
- `GET api.php?action=workflows` — list all workflows
- `GET api.php?action=workflows&id=X` — get single workflow with stages
- `POST api.php?action=workflows` — create workflow `{ name, slug, stages[] }`
- `PUT api.php?action=workflows&id=X` — update workflow `{ name, stages[] }`
- `DELETE api.php?action=workflows&id=X` — delete workflow (not in use)
- `POST api.php?action=workflows/transition` — transition item `{ item_id, to_stage, note }`
- `POST api.php?action=workflows/bulk-transition` — bulk transition `{ item_ids[], to_stage, note }`
- `GET api.php?action=workflows/history&item_id=X` — transition history for an item
- `GET api.php?action=workflows/for-collection&collection_id=X` — get workflow for a collection

CRUD endpoints require `settings.*` capability. Transition, history, and for-collection endpoints are available to any authenticated user (stage-level role enforcement is done in the handlers).

### Ranger Integration
Use the `manage_workflows` tool:
- "List all workflows"
- "Create a workflow called Legal Review with stages Draft, Legal, Approved, Published"
- "Move item 42 to the approved stage"
- "Show the transition history for item 15"

### Database Tables
- `workflows` — id, name, slug, stages (JSON), is_default, created_at, updated_at
- `workflow_transitions` — id, item_id, collection_id, from_stage, to_stage, user_id, note, created_at
- `collections.workflow_id` — foreign key to assign a workflow to a collection

### Files
- `php/workflows.php` — all backend handlers, migration, and helpers
- `src/pages/Workflows.svelte` — admin UI for managing workflows
- `src/pages/CollectionItems.svelte` — workflow-aware status badges and transitions
- `src/pages/CollectionList.svelte` — workflow selector in collection editor
- `src/components/RightSidebar.svelte` — editor transitions and history
- `src/components/Sidebar.svelte` — workflows nav link
- `src/lib/api.js` — `workflows` client object
- `php/ranger.php` — `manage_workflows` Ranger tool

## Releases (v3.1.0)

### What are Releases?
Releases let users bundle multiple content changes and publish them all at once. A release is like a staging area — create a draft release, add changes to it, preview everything as a bundle, then publish atomically in a single database transaction. Published releases can be rolled back to revert all changes.

### Use Cases
- **Campaign launches** — prepare homepage redesign + blog posts + navigation changes and publish everything at a specific time
- **Coordinated updates** — avoid a broken-looking site while making related changes across multiple pages
- **Safe rollback** — undo an entire batch of changes with one click if something goes wrong
- **Team coordination** — multiple people add changes to the same release, everything goes live together

### Release Lifecycle
- **Draft** — Changes can be added, removed, and the release can be edited or deleted
- **Published** — All changes applied atomically; can be rolled back
- **Rolled Back** — All changes reverted in reverse order; terminal state

### How to Use
1. Go to **Releases** in the sidebar
2. Click **+ New Release** and give it a name (e.g., "Spring Campaign")
3. Add changes — each content edit captures before/after snapshots
4. Review all changes in the release detail view
5. Click **Publish** — everything goes live atomically
6. If something's wrong, click **Rollback** — all changes revert instantly

### Entity Types
Changes can target: `field`, `item`, `page`, `menu`, `collection`

### Access Control
Releases are admin-only. Editors and developers cannot create, publish, or roll back releases.

### Using with Ranger
- "Create a release called Monday Launch"
- "Show me all my releases"
- "Publish the Spring Campaign release"
- "Roll back the last release"

### API Endpoints (admin-only, requires `settings.*` capability)
- `GET api.php?action=releases` — list all releases with change counts
- `GET api.php?action=releases&id=X` — get release with all its changes
- `POST api.php?action=releases` — create release `{ name, description }`
- `PUT api.php?action=releases&id=X` — update release name/description (draft only)
- `DELETE api.php?action=releases&id=X` — delete release (draft only)
- `POST api.php?action=releases/publish` — publish release `{ id }` — applies all changes atomically
- `POST api.php?action=releases/rollback` — rollback release `{ id }` — reverts all changes
