- `POST api.php?action=releases/changes` — add change `{ release_id, entity_type, entity_id, entity_name, action, snapshot_before, snapshot_after }`
- `DELETE api.php?action=releases/changes&id=X` — remove a change from a draft release

### Database Tables
- `releases` — id, name, description, status (draft/published/rolled_back), created_by, published_at, published_by, timestamps
- `release_changes` — id, release_id, entity_type, entity_id, entity_name, action (create/update/delete), snapshot_before (JSON), snapshot_after (JSON), created_at

### Files
- `php/releases.php` — all backend handlers and entity state helpers
- `src/pages/Releases.svelte` — admin UI
- `src/lib/api.js` — `releases` client object

## Ranger AI Assistant (v3.0.0)

### What is Ranger?
Ranger is an AI-powered assistant built into the Outpost admin panel sidebar. It provides a natural-language interface for every CMS operation — content editors can ask "create a blog post about our spring sale" and developers can ask "add a color field to the services collection." Ranger understands Outpost's template syntax, field types, collections, and architecture.

### Configuration
Configure Ranger at **Settings > Integrations**:
1. Choose an AI provider: Claude (Anthropic), OpenAI (GPT-4o/GPT-4), or Google (Gemini)
2. Enter your API key (encrypted at rest with AES-256-GCM)
3. Optionally select a model and output style (concise, detailed, casual, technical, or custom)
4. Click Save, then open Ranger via the sparkle icon in the top bar or "Ranger" in the sidebar

No Outpost account or subscription required — bring your own API key.

### Example Requests
- "Create a blog collection with title, excerpt, featured_image, and body fields"
- "Build me a restaurant site with homepage, menu, and contact pages"
- "Upload this image from Unsplash: [URL]"
- "Turn on dark mode"
- "Add a new admin user called Sarah"
- "Create a backup"
- "Set up a contact form with name, email, and message fields"
- "Check my templates for syntax errors"
- "Create a release called Monday Launch"

### Provider Comparison
- **Claude (Anthropic)** — Recommended. Best tool use, largest output, prompt caching saves money after the first message.
- **OpenAI (GPT)** — Solid alternative. Familiar if you use ChatGPT. GPT-4o is fast and capable.
- **Google Gemini** — Cheapest per token. Good for simple tasks like listing content or updating settings.

### Output Styles
- Concise — short, direct answers
- Detailed — thorough explanations with context
- Casual — friendly, conversational tone
- Technical — precise, developer-oriented language
- Custom — provide your own instructions

### Conversation History
Conversations are saved automatically. Access via the history icon in the Ranger panel. Shows total token usage and estimated cost per conversation. Conversations can be renamed, loaded, or deleted.

### API Endpoints

- `POST api.php?action=ranger/chat` — SSE streaming chat endpoint. Body: `{ "message": "...", "conversation_id": "...", "model": "...", "style": "..." }`. Returns Server-Sent Events with `type: "text"`, `type: "tool_call"`, `type: "tool_result"`, `type: "usage"`, and `type: "done"`.
- `GET api.php?action=ranger/conversations` — list all conversations for the current user (most recent first)
- `POST api.php?action=ranger/conversations` — create a new conversation. Body: `{ "title": "..." }`
- `PUT api.php?action=ranger/conversations&id=N` — update conversation (rename). Body: `{ "title": "..." }`
- `DELETE api.php?action=ranger/conversations&id=N` — delete a conversation and all its messages
- `GET api.php?action=ranger/conversations&id=N` — get a single conversation with all messages
- `GET api.php?action=ranger/settings` — get current Ranger configuration (provider, model, style)
- `PUT api.php?action=ranger/settings` — update Ranger configuration. Body: `{ "provider": "...", "api_key": "...", "model": "...", "style": "..." }`

### Available Tools (35 total)

**Content tools:**
- `create_page` — create a new page with path, title, and optional content
- `update_page` — update page fields, meta, or visibility
- `delete_page` — delete a page (homepage and globals protected)
- `list_pages` — list all pages with optional search
- `create_collection` — create a new collection with name, slug, URL pattern, and schema
- `update_collection` — update collection settings or schema
- `create_item` — create a collection item with field data
- `update_item` — update an existing collection item
- `delete_item` — delete a collection item
- `list_content` — list items from a collection with filtering and pagination
- `manage_globals` — get or set global field values

**Media tools:**
- `upload_media` — upload a file to the media library
- `upload_media_from_url` — download an image from a URL and add to media library

**Site file tools** (named `theme_file` for historical reasons; all operate on files at the site root):
- `create_theme_file` — create a new file at the site root
- `update_theme_file` — update file contents at the site root
- `delete_theme_file` — delete a file from the site root
- `read_file` — read the contents of a site file
- `list_theme_files` — list all files at the site root

**Schema tools:**
- `create_collection_field` — add a field to a collection's schema

**Configuration tools:**
- `update_settings` — update site settings (site name, tagline, email, etc.)
- `list_settings` — list current site settings
- `configure_email` — configure SMTP email settings
- `manage_navigation` — create, update, or list navigation menus and items

**User & member tools:**
- `manage_users` — list, create, update, or delete admin users
- `manage_members` — list, create, update, or delete site members

**Build tools:**
- `manage_forms` — create, update, list, or delete forms and view submissions
- `manage_channels` — create, update, list, or sync external data channels
- `manage_webhooks` — create, update, list, or delete webhooks
- `manage_folders` — create, update, list, or delete folders and labels
- `manage_backups` — create, list, download, or delete backups

**Developer tools:**
- `query_database` — run read-only SELECT queries against the SQLite database
- `debug_template` — check a template file for syntax errors and render issues
- `search_docs` — search the Outpost developer documentation
- `get_system_info` — get CMS version, PHP version, database stats

**Frontend tools:**
- `frontend_action` — toggle dark mode, open/close navigation, or trigger other UI actions

### How the Tool System Works

1. User sends a message via the chat interface
2. Backend classifies the message intent (content, build, UI, code, general)
3. Only relevant tools for that intent are sent to the AI provider (3–13 tools instead of all 35)
4. AI provider decides which tools to call based on the user's request
5. Backend executes each tool call with full capability and security checks
6. Tool results stream back to the client as SSE events
7. AI provider generates a final response incorporating tool results

**Conversation trimming:** Conversations over 20 messages are trimmed to first 2 + last 16 messages to prevent context overflow.

**Tool result truncation:** `read_file` capped at 2000 chars, `list_content` at 10 items, `search_docs` at 500 chars per result.

### Security Model

- **SSRF protection** — outbound HTTP requests (e.g., `upload_media_from_url`) validate URLs against private IP ranges (10.x, 172.16-31.x, 192.168.x, 127.x, ::1) and internal hostnames
- **Path traversal prevention** — file tools enforce site root boundaries; `../` and absolute paths outside the site root are blocked; writes inside `outpost/` are rejected
- **CSRF enforcement** — all POST/PUT/DELETE endpoints require a valid `X-CSRF-Token` header
- **Capability enforcement** — every tool checks the authenticated user's role capabilities before execution (e.g., editors cannot access `manage_users`)
- **XSS prevention** — tool outputs are HTML-escaped before rendering in the chat interface
- **SQL restrictions** — `query_database` allows only SELECT statements; CREATE, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, DROP, ALTER, and PRAGMA are blocked
- **API key encryption** — provider API keys are encrypted with AES-256-GCM using a site-specific key before storage
- **Error sanitization** — internal PHP errors, stack traces, and file paths are never sent to the client; logged server-side with generic client messages
- **Role scoping** — editors see ~13 content tools, developers see ~25 content+code tools, admins see all 35 tools

## Lodge (Member Portal)

Lodge turns any collection into a member-facing content portal. Members can create, edit, and delete their own items from the front end — with optional approval, tier gating, and field whitelisting.

### Enabling Lodge

Admin → Collections → [Collection] → Lodge tab. Toggle on and configure options.

### Configuration options

| Option | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `allow_create` | boolean | true | Members can create items |
| `allow_edit` | boolean | true | Members can edit own items |
| `allow_delete` | boolean | false | Members can delete own items |
| `require_approval` | boolean | false | Items held as `pending` until admin approves |
| `max_items_per_member` | integer | 0 (unlimited) | Max items per member |
| `editable_fields` | array | [] (all) | Whitelist of editable field slugs |
| `readonly_fields` | array | [] | Visible but not editable fields |
| `required_tiers` | array | [] (any) | Member tiers allowed access |

### Routing

Lodge has its own sub-router in `front-router.php`. All URLs live under a configurable slug (default: `/lodge`, changeable in Settings → `lodge_slug`).

| URL | Template | Injected params |
|---|---|---|
| `/lodge` | `lodge/dashboard.html` | — |
| `/lodge/edit/42` | `lodge/edit.html` | `$_GET['item_id'] = 42` |
| `/lodge/create/posts` | `lodge/create.html` | `$_GET['collection'] = 'posts'` |
| `/lodge/profile` | `lodge/profile.html` | — |

Templates go in the `lodge/` subdirectory at the site root. Falls back from `{custom-slug}/` to `lodge/`. All routes require member auth — unauthenticated users redirect to login.

### Lodge API endpoints (member-api.php)

All require member session (JWT or cookie).

| Action | Method | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `lodge/dashboard` | GET | Overview of member's Lodge activity |
| `lodge/items&collection=slug` | GET | Member's own items (paginated) |
| `lodge/items&collection=slug` | POST | Create new item |
| `lodge/items&id=123` | PUT | Update own item |
| `lodge/items&id=123` | DELETE | Delete own item (if allowed) |
| `lodge/profile` | GET | Member profile + tier + meta |
| `lodge/profile` | PUT | Update profile metadata |
| `lodge/upload&item_id=123` | POST | Upload file to own item |

### Template tags

- `<outpost-lodge-dashboard>` — member dashboard with item counts
- `<outpost-lodge-items collection="posts">` — list member's own items
- `<outpost-lodge-form collection="posts">` — create/edit form (respects editable_fields)
- `<outpost-lodge-form collection="posts" item="query:id">` — edit form (reads item ID from URL)

### Security

- Ownership scoping: all queries filter by `owner_member_id`
- Field whitelisting: only `editable_fields` accepted, others silently dropped
- Rate limiting per member session
- MIME validation on uploads
- Slug sanitization (no path traversal)
- XSS: member content sanitized before storage
- Tier enforcement via `required_tiers`

### Database changes (v4.6.0)
- `members.tier` — member tier column for granular access control
- `members.meta` — JSON column for arbitrary member metadata
- `items.owner_member_id` — links collection items to the creating member

### Feature Toggles (v4.6.0)
14 sidebar features can be individually toggled on/off via Admin > Settings > Features. Disabled features are hidden from the sidebar and their API endpoints return 403.

### Sidebar Accordion Groups (v4.6.0)
Admin sidebar organized into 5 collapsible groups: Content, Design, Connect, Members, System. Group state persists across sessions.

---

## Boost — Performance Suite (v4.9.0)

Comprehensive performance optimization for Outpost sites. Configure from Settings > Boost.

### Features

- **Developer Mode** — Single toggle at the top of the Boost settings. Disables all caching, compression, minification, and browser cache headers. Sends no-cache headers. The first check in every Boost function.
- **Page caching** — Full-page HTML cache for anonymous visitors. Cached pages served before any PHP/DB processing. Configurable TTL (default: 1 hour), glob-pattern path exclusions (default: `/outpost/*`, `/api/*`). Never caches: logged-in users, POST requests, previews, nocache param.
- **Cache preloading** — One-click button crawls all page and collection item URLs to warm the cache.
- **Browser cache headers** — Cache-Control, Expires, ETag, Last-Modified on static assets. Configurable TTL per type: CSS (1 year), JS (1 year), images (30 days), fonts (1 year). Hashed filenames marked `immutable`. Returns 304 Not Modified for unchanged files.
- **GZIP compression** — Compresses HTML responses, 60-80% size reduction. Requires PHP `zlib` extension. Falls back gracefully if unavailable.
- **HTML minification** — Strips whitespace and comments. Preserves `<pre>`, `<code>`, `<script>`, `<style>`, `<textarea>` content and Outpost block markers.
- **Lazy loading** — Auto-adds `loading="lazy"` to `<img>` and `<iframe>` tags. Configurable skip count for above-the-fold images (default: 2).
- **Database optimization** — Cleans expired rate limits, old activity logs (90d+), old analytics (1y+), stale preview tokens. Runs VACUUM.

### Configuration (stored as JSON in settings table, key: boost_config)

```json
{
  "enabled": true,
  "developer_mode": false,
  "page_cache": true,
  "page_cache_ttl": 3600,
  "page_cache_excludes": ["/outpost/*", "/api/*"],
  "browser_cache": true,
  "browser_cache_css_ttl": 31536000,
  "browser_cache_js_ttl": 31536000,
  "browser_cache_img_ttl": 2592000,
  "browser_cache_font_ttl": 31536000,
  "compression": true,
  "html_minify": true,
  "lazy_loading": true,
  "lazy_skip_count": 2
}
```

### API Endpoints (admin auth required)

- `GET boost/status` — cache stats, DB size, compression availability
- `GET boost/config` — current config
- `PUT boost/config` — update config (auto-clears page cache)
- `POST boost/clear-cache` — clear all page + template cache
- `POST boost/preload` — warm cache by crawling all URLs
- `POST boost/optimize-db` — cleanup + VACUUM

### Response Headers

- `X-Boost-Cache: HIT` or `MISS` — whether page was served from cache
- `X-Boost-Age: N` — cache age in seconds (on HIT only)

### Integration

Boost hooks into `front-router.php` early in the request lifecycle. Static assets get browser cache headers before being served. For HTML pages, Boost checks the page cache first (exits on HIT), then starts output buffering. The buffer callback applies lazy loading, minification, and compression, then caches the result. The legacy engine.php cache defers to Boost when it is loaded.

---

## Compass — Smart Filtering & Search (v4.8.0)

Data-attribute-driven filtering and search for any collection. You write your own HTML with your own classes and layout. Add `data-compass` attributes to make elements smart. Compass handles the filtering behavior — you control everything visual. No forced layouts, no generated wrappers.

### How It Works

1. Compass JS discovers all `[data-compass]` elements on the page
2. Groups them by `data-collection` into filter sets
3. Binds appropriate event listeners (change, input, click)
4. When any filter changes, fetches results from `compass/filter` API
5. Updates results container, counts, and pager
6. Syncs state to URL query string

### Quick Start

```html
<input type="text" data-compass="search" data-collection="posts" placeholder="Search...">

<select data-compass="dropdown" data-collection="posts" data-source="folder:categories">
  <option value="">All Categories</option>
</select>

<div data-compass="results" data-collection="posts">
  <outpost-each collection="posts" limit="12">
    <outpost-include partial="post-card"></outpost-include>
  </outpost-each>
</div>
```

### All Data Attributes

- `data-compass="search"` on `<input>` — Text search, debounced 300ms
- `data-compass="dropdown"` on `<select>` — Single-select filter. Auto-populates options if empty.
- `data-compass="checkbox"` on `<div>` — Multi-select checkboxes. Auto-fills from source.
- `data-compass="radio"` on `<div>` — Single-select radio buttons. Auto-fills from source.
- `data-compass="range-min"` on `<input type="range">` — Low end of range filter
- `data-compass="range-max"` on `<input type="range">` — High end of range filter
- `data-compass="az"` on `<div>` — A-Z letter buttons. Auto-generated.
- `data-compass="toggle"` on `<input type="checkbox">` — Boolean label/field filter
- `data-compass="proximity"` on `<button>` — Geolocation "near me" trigger
- `data-compass="results"` on `<div>` — Results container. Content replaced on filter.
- `data-compass="count"` on any element — Text replaced with result count
- `data-compass="pager"` on `<div>` — Pagination buttons auto-generated
- `data-compass="reset"` on `<button>` — Clears all filters
- `data-compass="selections"` on `<div>` — Active filter pills
- `data-compass="sort"` on `<select>` — Sort order selector
- `data-compass="submit"` on `<button>` — Triggers filter on click

### Supporting Attributes

- `data-collection` (all) — Collection slug to filter
- `data-source` (dropdown, checkbox, radio, toggle) — `folder:slug`, `field:name`, or `label:slug`
- `data-fields` (search) — Comma-separated fields to search
- `data-radius` (proximity) — Default radius in miles/km
- `data-unit` (proximity) — `miles` or `km`
- `data-per-page` (pager) — Results per page

### Source Types

- `folder:categories` — options from folder labels
- `field:city` — unique values from a data field
- `label:open-late` — single label toggle

### Legacy Syntax

The `<outpost-compass>` custom element tags still work — they compile to the same data-attribute HTML. Use them for quick prototyping; use raw data attributes for full control.

### API Endpoints

- `GET api.php?action=compass/values&collection=slug&source=folder:categories` — returns filter options with counts
- `POST api.php?action=compass/filter` — execute filter query (JSON body with collection, filters, sort, page, per_page)
- `GET api.php?action=compass/proximity&collection=slug&lat=X&lng=Y&radius=25&unit=mi` — geo search sorted by distance
- `GET api.php?action=compass/hierarchy&collection=slug&source=folder:categories` — nested category tree with counts
- `POST api.php?action=compass/reindex&collection=slug` — rebuild search index (admin auth required)

### URL State

Filters sync to URL query params: `q` (search), `{folder-slug}` or `{field-name}` (dropdown/checkbox/radio), `{field}_min`/`{field}_max` (range), `letter` (az), `lat`/`lng`/`radius` (proximity), `sort`, `page`. URL params pre-populate filters on page load; changes update URL via pushState.

### Indexing

SQLite `compass_index` table. Rebuilds automatically on item create/update/delete and label changes. First page load with Compass elements triggers initial build. Incremental updates for single-item changes. Sub-50ms queries on 10k+ items.

### Styling

Compass does not wrap your HTML. Your inputs, selects, buttons keep their markup and classes. Compass only injects minimal CSS for auto-generated content: checkbox/radio labels, letter buttons, pager buttons, and filter pills inside their respective containers. Override with CSS custom properties: `--compass-accent`, `--compass-pill-bg`, `--compass-pill-color`, `--compass-radius`, `--compass-gap`.

### Example: Full Directory Page

```html
<div class="directory">
  <header class="directory-header">
    <input type="text" class="search-input" data-compass="search" data-collection="business" data-fields="title,description,address" placeholder="Search businesses...">
    <select class="filter-select" data-compass="dropdown" data-collection="business" data-source="folder:categories">
      <option value="">All Categories</option>
    </select>
    <select class="filter-select" data-compass="dropdown" data-collection="business" data-source="field:city">
      <option value="">All Cities</option>
    </select>
  </header>

  <div class="toolbar">
    <span data-compass="count" data-collection="business">0 results</span>
    <div data-compass="selections" data-collection="business"></div>
    <button data-compass="reset" data-collection="business">Clear all</button>
    <select data-compass="sort" data-collection="business">
      <option value="title:asc">A-Z</option>
      <option value="created_at:desc">Newest</option>
    </select>
  </div>

  <aside class="sidebar">
    <div data-compass="checkbox" data-collection="business" data-source="folder:amenities"></div>
    <input type="range" data-compass="range-min" data-collection="business" data-source="field:rating" min="1" max="5">
    <input type="range" data-compass="range-max" data-collection="business" data-source="field:rating" min="1" max="5">
    <label><input type="checkbox" data-compass="toggle" data-collection="business" data-source="field:featured"> Featured Only</label>
    <button data-compass="proximity" data-collection="business" data-radius="25" data-unit="miles">Near Me</button>
  </aside>

  <div data-compass="results" data-collection="business">
    <outpost-each collection="business" limit="12">
      <article class="card">
        <img data-outpost="featured_image" data-type="image" src="placeholder.jpg">
        <h3 data-outpost="title">Business Name</h3>
        <p data-outpost="description">Description</p>
        <a data-bind="href:url">View Details</a>
      </article>
    </outpost-each>
  </div>

  <div data-compass="pager" data-collection="business" data-per-page="12"></div>
</div>
```

---

## Redirects (v4.10.0)

URL redirect management from the admin panel. Access from Tools > Redirects (requires `settings.*` capability).

### Match Types (evaluated in order)

1. **Exact match** — source URL must match request path exactly (trailing slashes normalized).
2. **Wildcard match** — source ends with `*`. The prefix is matched, and the remainder is appended to the target. Example: `/old-blog/*` → `/blog/*` makes `/old-blog/my-post` → `/blog/my-post`.
3. **Regex match** — source starts with `~`. Standard PCRE pattern. Capture groups `$1`, `$2`, etc. substituted in target. Example: `~^/post/(\d+)$` → `/archive/$1`.

### Redirect Types

- **301** — Permanent. Search engines transfer link equity. Default.
- **302** — Temporary. Search engines keep the original indexed.
- **307** — Temporary, preserves HTTP method. POST stays POST.

### Features

- **Hit tracking** — counter and last-hit timestamp per redirect.
- **Enable/disable** — toggle without deleting.
- **CSV import** — columns: source, target, type (optional, defaults to 301). Header row auto-detected.
- **URL test tool** — check how a URL would be handled without navigating.
- **Notes** — optional per-redirect notes for your reference.

### Security

- **Open redirect prevention** — targets validated at execution time. Only relative paths and same-domain absolute URLs allowed. External domains and protocol-relative URLs (`//`) are blocked.
- **Auth** — all management endpoints require `settings.*` capability (admin role or higher).

### API Endpoints

| Method | Endpoint | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|
| GET | `api.php?action=redirects` | List all redirects |
| POST | `api.php?action=redirects` | Create a redirect |
| PUT | `api.php?action=redirects&id=ID` | Update a redirect |
| DELETE | `api.php?action=redirects&id=ID` | Delete a redirect |
| POST | `api.php?action=redirects/test` | Test a URL against rules |
| POST | `api.php?action=redirects/import` | Bulk import from CSV |

### How It Works

The redirect check runs in `front-router.php` after config/database load but before theme routing. The `redirects` table is auto-created on first use. Evaluation order: exact → wildcard → regex. First match wins. On match, the configured status code and Location header are sent immediately.

---

## Shield — Security Suite (v4.9.0)

Comprehensive security hardening system. Enabled by default, configurable from Settings > Shield.

### Seven layers of protection

1. **Login Protection** — Lockout after N failed attempts (default: 5 attempts, 15 min lockout). Auto-permanent-block after repeated lockouts (default: 3 lockout events). Configurable via `login_max_attempts`, `login_lockout_minutes`, `auto_block_after_lockouts`.

2. **IP Blocklist** — Manual and automatic IP blocking. Checked on every request before routing. Temporary blocks auto-expire. Admin cannot block own IP. `shield_blocked_ips` table with `ip`, `reason`, `auto_blocked`, `blocked_at`, `expires_at`.

3. **WAF-lite Firewall** — Scans URLs, query strings, and POST bodies for attack patterns:
   - `sql_injection` — UNION SELECT, DROP TABLE, SLEEP(), tautologies, comment injection
   - `xss` — script tags, javascript: URLs, inline event handlers
   - `path_traversal` — ../ and URL-encoded variants
   - `php_injection` — <?php, eval(), base64_decode(), system(), exec(), etc.
   - `null_byte` — %00 injection
   Two modes: "block" (403 + log) and "log" (log only, for monitoring). Skips logged-in admin sessions.

4. **File Integrity Monitoring** — MD5 hashes of core PHP files. First scan stores baseline. Subsequent scans detect modified or deleted files. On-demand check from admin UI.

5. **Security Headers** — X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN, X-XSS-Protection: 1; mode=block, Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin, Permissions-Policy: camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=().

6. **Traffic Logging** — Last 1,000 requests with IP, path, method, user agent, timestamp. Auto-pruned.

7. **Email Notifications** — Alerts for lockout, permanent block, firewall block, file integrity change. Uses configured SMTP settings.

### Configuration

Stored as JSON in `settings` table under key `shield_config`:

```json
{
  "enabled": true,
  "login_lockout": true,
  "login_max_attempts": 5,
  "login_lockout_minutes": 15,
  "auto_block_after_lockouts": 3,
  "firewall_enabled": true,
  "firewall_mode": "block",
  "file_integrity": true,
  "security_headers": true,
  "traffic_logging": true,
  "email_notifications": false,
  "notification_email": ""
}
```

### API Endpoints (admin auth required)

- `GET shield/status` — dashboard: blocked IPs count, attacks in 24h, failed logins, last integrity check
- `GET shield/log?limit=50&offset=0` — security event log (paginated)
- `GET shield/blocked-ips` — list all blocked IPs
- `POST shield/block-ip` — block IP. Body: `{ "ip": "...", "reason": "..." }`
- `DELETE shield/block-ip&ip=X` — unblock IP
- `GET shield/traffic?limit=100&offset=0` — recent traffic (paginated)
- `POST shield/file-check` — run file integrity check
- `GET shield/config` — get Shield config
- `PUT shield/config` — update Shield config
- `GET shield/login-attempts?limit=50` — recent login attempts

### Database Tables (auto-created)

- `shield_login_attempts` — id, ip, username, success, created_at
- `shield_blocked_ips` — id, ip (UNIQUE), reason, auto_blocked, blocked_at, expires_at
- `shield_log` — id, ip, path, method, rule, payload, blocked, created_at
- `shield_file_hashes` — file_path (PK), hash, checked_at
- `shield_traffic` — id, ip, path, method, status_code, user_agent, threat, created_at
